925.text: On an application for judgment for delivery up of goods where the defendant will not be given the alternative of paying their value, the evidence must identify the goods and state where the claimant believes the goods to be situated and why their specific delivery up is sought.

928.text: Where default judgment is given on a claim for a sum of money expressed in a currency other than US Dollars, the judgment should be for the amount of that currency with the addition of ‘or the US Dollar equivalent at the time of payment’.

934.text: CASES WHERE THE COURT MUST SET ASIDE JUDGMENT ENTERED UNDER PART 13

936.text: The Court must set aside a judgment entered under Part 13 if judgment was wrongly entered because.

937.text: (1) in the case of a judgment in default of an acknowledgment of service, any of the conditions in Rules 13.4 and 13.6 was not satisfied;

938.text: (2) in the case of a judgment in default of a defence, any of the conditions in Rules 13.5 and 13.6 was not satisfied; or

939.text: (3) the whole of the claim was satisfied before judgment was entered.

940.text: Cases Where The Court May Set Aside Or Vary Judgment Entered Under Part 13

941.text: 14.2 In any other case, the Court may, on such conditions as it sees fit, set aside or vary a judgment entered

942.text: PART 14 Setting Aside Or Varying Default Judgment

944.text: (1) the defendant has a real prospect of successfully defending the claim; or

945.text: (2) it appears to the Court that there is some other good reason why:

946.text: (a) the judgment should be set aside or varied; or

947.text: (b) the defendant should be allowed to defend the claim.

949.text: In considering whether to set aside or vary a judgment entered under Part 13, the matters to which the Court must have regard include whether the person seeking to set aside the judgment made an application to do so promptly.

950.text: Application To Set Aside Or Vary Judgment — Procedure

952.text: An application under Rule 14.2 (cases where the Court may set aside or vary judgment) must be supported by evidence.

956.text:
15.1   
A party may admit the truth of the whole or any part of another party’s case by giving notice in writing (such as in a statement of case or by letter).

958.text: Where the only remedy which the claimant is seeking is the payment of money, the defendant may also make an admission in accordance with:

959.text: (1) Rule 15.14 (admission of whole claim for specified amount of money); (2) Rule 15.18 (admission of part of claim for specified amount of money); (3) Rule 15.19 (admission of liability to pay whole of claim for unspecified amount of money); or

960.text: (4) Rule 15.24 (admission of liability to pay claim for unspecified amount of money where defendant offers a sum in satisfaction of the claim).

962.text: Where the defendant makes an admission as mentioned in Rule 15.2, the claimant has a right to enter judgment.

964.text: The permission of the Court is required to amend or withdraw an admission.

967.text: When the claim form is served on a defendant, the forms for responding to the claim that will accompany them will include a form for making an admission.

969.text: If the defendant wishes to make an admission in respect of the whole of a claim for a specified amount of money, the admission form or other written notice of the admission should be completed and returned to the claimant within the period set out in Rule 15.8.

971.text: If the defendant wishes to make an admission in respect of a part of a claim for a specified amount of money, or in respect of a claim for an unspecified amount of money:

972.text: (1) the admission form or other written notice of admission should be completed and filed with the Court within the period set out in Rule 15.8; and

973.text: (2) the defendant may also file a defence under Rule 16.3.

976.text: The period for returning an admission under Rule 15.14 or for filing it under Rules 15.18, 15.19 or 15.24 is:

977.text: (1) where the defendant is served with a claim form which states that particulars of claim will follow, 14 days after service of the particulars; and

978.text: (2) in any other case, 14 days after service of the claim form.

980.text: Rule 15.8 is subject to the following rules:

981.text: (1) Rule 9.56 (which specifies how the period for filing or returning an admission is calculated where the

982.text: claim form is served out of the jurisdiction); and

983.text: (2) Rule 9.48 (which requires the Court to specify the period for responding to the particulars of claim when it makes an order under that rule).

985.text: A defendant may return an admission under Rule 15.14 or file it under Rules 15.18, 15.19 or 15.24 after the end of the period for returning or filing it specified in Rule 15.8 if the claimant has not obtained default judgment under Part 13.

987.text: If he does so, this Part shall apply as if he had made the admission within that period.

988.text: Admission By Notice In Writing — Application For Judgment

989.text:
15.12   
Where a party makes an admission under Rule 15.1 (admission by notice in writing), any other party may apply for judgment on the admission.

990.text: 15.13 Judgment shall be such as it appears to the Court that the applicant is entitled to on the admission.

991.text: Admission Of Whole Of Claim For Specified Amount Of Money

994.text: (1) the only remedy which the claimant is seeking is the payment of a specified amount of money; and (2) the defendant admits the whole of the claim the defendant may admit the claim by returning to the claimant an admission in Form 15/01 or Form 15/02.

996.text: The claimant may obtain judgment by filing a request in Form 13/01, and, if he does so:

997.text:
(1) if the defendant has not requested time to pay, the procedure in Rules 15.16 to 15.17 will apply;   
(2) if the defendant has requested time to pay, the procedure in Rules 15.33 to 15.38 will apply.

999.text: The claimant may specify in his request for judgment:

1000.text: (1) the date by which the whole of the judgment debt is to be paid; or

1001.text: (2) the times and rate at which it is to be paid by instalments.

1003.text: On receipt of the request for judgment the Court will enter judgment for the amount of the claim (less any payments made) and costs:

1004.text: (1) to be paid by the date or at the rate specified in the request for judgment; or (2) if none is specified, immediately.

1005.text: Admission Of Part Of A Claim For A Specified Amount Of Money

1007.text: If the defendant admits part of a claim for a specified amount of money, the claimant may apply under Rule 15.12 for judgment on the admission.

1008.text: Admission Of Liability To Pay Whole Of Claim For Unspecified Amount Of Money

1011.text: (1) the only remedy which the claimant is seeking is the payment of money;

1012.text: (2) the amount of the claim is not specified; and

1013.text: (3) the defendant admits liability but does not offer to pay a specified amount of money in satisfaction of the claim,

1014.text: the defendant may admit the claim by filing an admission in the relevant Form P15/03.

1016.text: On receipt of the admission, the Court will serve a copy on the claimant.

1018.text: The claimant may obtain judgment by filing a request in Form P15/03.

1020.text: If the claimant does not file a request for judgment within 14 days after service of the admission on him, the claim is stayed until he files the request.

1022.text: On receipt of the request for judgment the Court will enter judgment for an amount to be decided by the Court and costs.

1023.text: Admission Of Liability To Pay Claim For Unspecified Amount Of Money Where Defendant Offers A Sum In Satisfaction Of The Claim

1026.text: (1) the only remedy which the claimant is seeking is the payment of money;

1027.text: (2) the amount of the claim is not specified; and

1030.text: (b) offers to pay a specified amount of money in satisfaction of the claim

1031.text: the defendant may admit the claim by filing an admission in Form P15/01.

1033.text: On receipt of the admission, the Court will serve a notice on the claimant requiring him to return the notice stating whether or not he accepts the amount in satisfaction of the claim.

1035.text: If the claimant does not file the notice within 14 days after it is served on him, the claim is stayed until he files the notice.

1037.text: If the claimant accepts the offer he may obtain judgment by filing a request in Form P13/01 and if he does so:

1038.text:
(1) if the defendant has not requested time to pay, the procedure in Rules 15.28 to 15.31 will apply;   
(2) if the defendant has requested time to pay, the procedure in Rules 15.33 to 15.38 will apply.

1040.text: The claimant may specify in his request for judgment:

1041.text: (1) the date by which the whole of the judgment debt is to be paid; or

1042.text: (2) the times and rate at which it is to be paid by instalments.

1044.text: On receipt of the request for judgment, the Court will enter judgment for the amount offered by the defendant (less any payments made) and costs:

1045.text: (1) to be paid on the date or at the rate specified in the request for judgment; or

1046.text: (2) if no such date or rate is specified, to be paid immediately.

1048.text: If the claimant does not accept the amount offered by the defendant, he may obtain judgment by filing a request in Form P15/03.

1050.text: Judgment under Rule 15.30 will be for an amount to be decided by the Court and costs.

1051.text: Directions In Relation To Outstanding Matters

1053.text: Where the Court enters judgment under Rules 15.23 or 15.30 for an amount to be decided by the Court, it will give any directions it considers appropriate.

1056.text: A defendant who makes an admission under Rules 15.14, 15.18 or 15.24 (admission relating to a claim for a specified amount of money or offering to pay a specified amount of money) may make a request for time to pay.

1058.text: A request for time to pay is a proposal about the date of payment or a proposal to pay by instalments at the times and rate specified in the request.

1060.text: If the defendant is requesting time to pay he should complete as fully as possible the statement of means contained in the admission form, or otherwise give in writing the same details of his means as could have been given in the admission form.

1062.text: If the claimant accepts the defendant’s request, he may obtain judgment by filing a request in Form P13/01.

1064.text: On receipt of the request for judgment, the Court will enter judgment:

1065.text: (1) where Rule 15.14 applies, for the amount of the claim (less any payments made) and costs;

1066.text: (2) where Rule 15.18 applies, for the amount admitted (less any payments made) and costs; or

1067.text: (3) where Rule 15.24 applies, for the amount offered by the defendant (less any payments made) and

1069.text: (4) (in all cases) for payment at the time and rate specified in the defendant’s request for time to pay.

1072.text: Where the defendant has made a request for time to pay under Rule 15.33 if the claimant does not accept the defendant’s proposals for payment, he must file a notice in Form P13/01.

1074.text: Where the defendant’s admission was served direct on the claimant , a copy of the admission and the request for time to pay must be filed with the claimant’s notice.

1076.text: When the Court receives the claimant’s notice, it will enter judgment for the amount admitted (less any payments made) to be paid at the time and rate of payment determined by the Registrar.

1078.text: Where the Registrar is to determine the time and rate of payment, he may do so without a hearing.

1080.text: If there is to be a hearing to determine the time and rate of payment, the Court must give each party at least 7 days’ notice of the hearing.

1082.text: Where the Registrar has determined the time and rate of payment under Rule 15.42, either party may apply for the decision to be re-determined by a Judge.

1084.text: An application for re-determination must be made within 14 days after service of the determination on the applicant.

1086.text: In deciding the time and rate of payment the Court will take into account:

1087.text: (1) the defendant’s statement of means set out in the admission form or in any other written notice of the admission filed,

1088.text: (2) the claimant’s objections to the defendant’s request set out in the claimant’s notice, and

1091.text: Either party may, on account of a change in circumstances since the date of the decision (or redetermination as the case may be) apply to vary the time and rate of payment of instalments still remaining unpaid.

1093.text: An application to vary under Rule 15.47 above should be made in accordance with Part 23.

1096.text: Judgment under Rule 15.14 (admission of whole of claim for specified amount of money) shall include the amount of interest claimed to the date of judgment if:

1097.text:
(1) the claim form includes the details required by Rule 17.18;   
(2) where interest is claimed under (a) Articles 118 or 119 of the Contract Law 2004; (b) Articles 17, 18 or 32 of the Law of Damages and Remedies 2005; (c) Article 121(b) of the Law of Obligations 2005; or (d) Article 80 of the DIFC Employment Law 2005;

1098.text: the rate is no higher than the rate of interest fixed by these Rules under Article 39(2)(a) of the Court Law payable on judgment debts at the date when the claim form was issued; and

1099.text: (3) the claimant’s request for judgment includes a calculation of the interest claimed for the period from the date up to which interest was stated to be calculated in the claim form to the date of the request for judgment.

1101.text: In any case where judgment is entered under Rule 15.14 and the conditions in Rule 15.49 are not satisfied judgment shall be for an amount of interest to be decided by the Court.

1103.text: Where judgment is entered for an amount of interest to be decided by the Court, the Court will give directions for the management of the case.

1106.text: An admission made under this Part may be withdrawn with the Court’s permission.

1108.text: In deciding whether to give permission for an admission to be withdrawn, the Court will have regard to all the circumstances of the case, including:

1109.text: (1) the grounds upon which the applicant seeks to withdraw the admission including whether or not new evidence has come to light which was not available at the time the admission was made;

1110.text: (2) the conduct of the parties, including any conduct which led the party making the admission to do so;

1111.text: (3) the prejudice that may be caused to any person if the admission is withdrawn;

1112.text: (4) the prejudice that may be caused to any person if the application is refused;

1113.text: (5) the stage in the proceedings at which the application to withdraw is made, in particular in relation to the date or period fixed for trial;

1114.text: (6) the prospects of success (if the admission is withdrawn) of the claim or part of the claim in relation to which the offer was made; and

1115.text: (7) the interests of the administration of justice.

1121.text: This Part does not apply where the claimant uses the procedure set out in Part 8 (alternative procedure for claims).

1123.text: In relation to specialist proceedings in respect of which special provisions for defence and reply are made by these Rules and Practice Directions applicable to those claims, the provisions of this Part apply only to the extent that they are not inconsistent with those Rules and Practice Directions.

1126.text: A defendant who wishes to defend all or part of a claim must file a defence.

1127.text: Consequence Of Not Filing A Defence

1128.text: 16.4 If a defendant fails to file a defence, the claimant may obtain default judgment if Part 13 allows it.

1130.text: 16.5 A party may file a defence by submitting it electronically using the Court ’s e-filing system.

1132.text: 16.6 Part 22 requires a defence to be verified by a statement of truth.

1134.text: The form of the statement of truth is as follows:

1135.text: ‘[I believe][the defendant believes] that the facts stated in this defence are true.’

1137.text: Attention is drawn to Section VI of Part 29 which sets out the consequences of verifying a statement of case containing a false statement without an honest belief in its truth.

1138.text: The Period For Filing A Defence

1140.text: The general rule is that the period for filing a defence is:

1141.text:
(1) 14 days after service of the particulars of claim; or   
(2) if the defendant files an acknowledgment of service under Part 11, 28 days after service of the particulars of claim.

1143.text: The general rule is subject to the following rules:

1144.text: (1) Rule 9.57 (which specifies how the period for filing a defence is calculated where the claim form is served out of the jurisdiction );

1145.text: (2) Rule 12.10 (which provides that, where the defendant makes an application disputing the Court’s

1146.text: jurisdiction , he need not file a defence before the hearing);

1147.text: (3) Rule 24.5 (which provides that, if the claimant applies for immediate judgment before the defendant has filed a defence, the defendant need not file a defence before the immediate judgment hearing); and

1148.text: (4) Rule 9.48 (which requires the Court to specify the period for responding to the particulars of claim when it makes an order under Rule 9.45).

1149.text: Agreement Extending The Period For Filing A Defence

1151.text: The defendant and the claimant may agree that the period for filing a defence specified in Rules 16.9 and 16.10 shall be extended by up to 28 days.

1153.text: Where the defendant and the claimant agree to extend the period for filing a defence, the defendant must notify the Court in writing.

1156.text: A copy of the defence must be served on every other party.

1158.text: 16.14 Part 21 applies to a defendant who wishes to make a counterclaim .

1160.text: Where a defendant to a claim serves a counterclaim under Part 21, the defence and counterclaim should normally form one document, with the counterclaim following on from the defence.

1163.text: If a claimant files a reply to the defence, he must:

1164.text: (1) file his reply within 21 days after service of the defence; and

1165.text: (2) serve it on all other parties at the same time.

1167.text: The reply should be served before case management information sheets are provided to the Court . In the normal case, this will allow the parties to consider any reply before completing the case management

1168.text: information sheet, and allow time for the preparation of the case memorandum and the list of issues each of which is required for the case management conference.

1170.text: In some cases, more than 21 days may be needed for the preparation, service and filing of a reply. In such cases an application should be made on paper for an extension of time and for a postponement of the case management conference.

1172.text: Where a claimant serves a reply and a defence to counterclaim , the reply and defence to counterclaim should normally form one document, with the defence to counterclaim following on from the reply.

1173.text: No Statement Of Case After A Reply To Be Filed Without Court’S Permission

1175.text: A party may not file or serve any statement of case after a reply without the permission of the Court .

1176.text: Claimant’S Notice Where Defence Is That Money Claimed Has Been Paid

1179.text: (1) the only claim (apart from a claim for costs and interest) is for a specified amount of money; and

1180.text: (2) the defendant states in his defence that he has paid to the claimant the amount claimed,

1181.text: the Court will send notice to the claimant requiring him to state in writing whether he wishes the proceedings to continue.

1183.text: The claimant must respond to a notice issued by the Court under Rule 16.21 within 28 days after service of the notice on him. When the claimant responds, he must serve a copy of his response on the defendant .

1185.text: If the claimant fails to respond under Rule 16.22 within 28 days after service of the Court’s notice on him, the claim shall be stayed .

1187.text: Where a claim is stayed under this Rule, any party may apply for the stay to be lifted.

1188.text: Claim Stayed If It Is Not Defended Or Admitted

1191.text: (1) at least 6 months have expired since the end of the period for filing a defence specified in Rules 16.9 and 16.10;

1192.text: (2) no defendant has served or filed an admission or filed a defence or counterclaim ; and

1193.text: (3) the claimant has not entered or applied for judgment under Part 13 (default judgment ), or Part 24 (immediate judgment);

1196.text: Where a claim is stayed under Rule 16.25, any party may apply for the stay to be lifted.

1199.text: Any application made under Rule 16.24 or Rule 16.26 should be made in accordance with Part 23 and should give the reason for the applicant’s delay in proceeding with or responding to the claim.

1204.text: This Part does not apply where the claimant uses the procedure set out in Part 8 (alternative procedure for claims).

1206.text: Where special provisions about statements of case are made by the Rules and Practice Directions applying to particular types of proceedings, the provisions of this Part apply only to the extent that they are not inconsistent with those Rules and Practice Directions.

1208.text: The Court may at any time before or after the issue of the claim form order a claim to proceed without the filing or service of statements of case.

1211.text: The claim form must be headed with the title of the proceedings, including the full name of each party. The full name means, in each case where it is known:

1212.text: (1) in the case of an individual, his full unabbreviated name and title by which he is known;

1213.text: (2) in the case of an individual carrying on business in a name other than his own name, the full unabbreviated name of the individual, together with the title by which he is known, and the full trading name;

1214.text: (3) in the case of a partnership (other than a limited liability partnership (LLP))—

1215.text: (a) where partners are being sued in the name of the partnership, the full name by which the partnership is known, together with the words $' ( \mathsf { A F i r m } ) ^ { \prime }$ ; or

1216.text: (b) where partners are being sued as individuals, the full unabbreviated name of each partner and the title by which he is known;

1217.text: (4) in the case of a company or limited liability partnership registered in the DIFC, the full registered name, including suffix (LLC, limited, LLP, etc), if any;

1218.text: (5) in the case of any other company or corporation, the full name by which it is known, including suffix where appropriate.

1219.text: Claimant’S Address To Be Included In The Claim Form

1221.text: The claim form must include the claimant’s mailing address or an address at which the claimant resides or carries on business. This Rule applies even though the claimant’s address for service is the business address of his legal representative .

1223.text: Where the defendant is an individual, the claimant should (if he is able to do so) include in the claim form the defendant’s mailing address or an address at which the defendant resides or carries on business. This Rule applies even though the defendant’s legal representative has agreed to accept service on the defendant’s behalf.

1225.text: If the claim form does not show an address as required by Rule 17.5, the claim form will not be issued. The Court will notify the claimant .

1231.text: In proceedings against the Government , the Centre Authority or any of the Centre’s Bodies, the claim form must comply with the requirements of Part 41.

1233.text: If the particulars of claim are not contained in, or are not served with the claim form, the claimant must state on the claim form that the particulars of claim will follow if an acknowledgement of service is filed which indicates an intention to defend the claim.

1235.text: If the claimant is claiming in a representative capacity, the claim form must state what that capacity is.

1237.text: If the defendant is sued in a representative capacity, the claim form must state what that capacity is.

1239.text: The Court may grant any remedy to which the claimant is entitled even if that remedy is not specified in the claim form.

1240.text: Contents Of The Particulars Of Claim — General

1242.text: A claimant is not required to serve particulars of claim unless and until the defendant serves an acknowledgment of service in response to the claim form.

1244.text: Where the claimant does not include the particulars of claim in the claim form, particulars of claim may be served separately:

1245.text: (1) either at the same time as the claim form, or (2) within 28 days of the filing of an acknowledgment of service which indicates an intention to defend.

1247.text: Particulars of claim served separately from the claim form must also contain:

1248.text:
(1) [deleted],   
(2) the claim number,   
(3) the title of the proceedings, and (4) the claimant’s address for service .

1253.text: If the claimant is seeking interest he must:

1254.text: (1) state whether he is doing so:

1255.text: (a) under the terms of a contract; (b) under an enactment and if so which; or (c) on some other basis and if so what that basis is; and (2) if the claim is for a specified amount of money, state:

1257.text: Contents Of The Particulars Of Claim — Specific

1259.text: Where a claim is made for an injunction or declaration in respect of or relating to any land the particulars of claim must:

1260.text: (1) state whether or not the injunction or declaration relates to residential premises, and (2) identify the land (by reference to a plan where necessary).

1262.text: Where a claim is brought to enforce a right to recover possession of goods the particulars of claim must contain a statement showing the value of the goods.

1264.text: Where a claim is brought for personal injury:

1265.text: (1) the particulars of claim must contain:

1266.text: (a) the claimant’s date of birth, and

1267.text: (b) brief details of the claimant’s personal injuries;

1268.text: (2) the claimant must attach to his particulars of claim a schedule of details of any past and future expenses and losses which he claims; and

1269.text: (3) where the claimant is relying on the evidence of a medical practitioner the claimant must attach to or serve with his particulars of claim a report from a medical practitioner about the personal injuries which he alleges in his claim.

1270.text: Matters Which Must Be Specifically Set Out In The Particulars Of Claim If Relied On

1272.text: A claimant who wishes to rely on:

1273.text: (1) a Certificate of Contravention under Article 55 of the Markets Law 2004; or (2) a Certificate setting out a decision of the Financial Markets Tribunal under Article 34(6) of the Regulatory Law 2004; must include in his particulars of claim a statement to that effect and give the following details:

1274.text:
(a) the type of finding and its date;   
(b) the issue in the claim to which it relates.

1277.text: If the defendant has not filed an acknowledgment of service under Part 11, he must give an address for service .

1279.text: Where the defendant is an individual, and the claim form does not contain an address for the defendant in accordance with Rule 17.6, or contains an incorrect address, the defendant must provide such an address in the defence.

1281.text: Where the defendant’s address for service is not the defendant’s mailing address or the address where he resides or carries on business, the defendant must still provide the address required by Rule 17.24.

1283.text: In his defence, the defendant must state:

1284.text:
(1) which of the allegations in the particulars of claim he denies;   
(2) which allegations he is unable to admit or deny, but which he requires the claimant to prove; and (3) which allegations he admits.

1286.text: Where the defendant denies an allegation:

1287.text:
(1) he must state his reasons for doing so; and   
(2) if he intends to put forward a different version of events from that given by the claimant , he must state his own version.

1290.text: (1) fails to deal with an allegation; but (2) has set out in his defence the nature of his case in relation to the issue to which that allegation is relevant, shall be taken to require that allegation to be proved.

1292.text: Where the claim includes a money claim, a defendant shall be taken to require that any allegation relating to the amount of money claimed be proved unless he expressly admits the allegation.

1294.text: Subject to Rules 17.28 and 17.29, a defendant who fails to deal with an allegation shall be taken to admit that allegation.

1296.text: If the defendant is defending in a representative capacity, he must state what that capacity is.

1298.text: The defendant must give details of the expiry of any relevant limitation period relied on.

1302.text: (1) contends he is entitled to money from the claimant ; and (2) relies on this as a defence to the whole or part of the claim,

1303.text: the contention may be included in the defence and set off against the claim, whether or not it is also a counterclaim .

1306.text: A claimant who does not file a reply to the defence shall not be taken to admit the matters raised in the defence.

1309.text: (1) files a reply to a defence; but

1310.text: (2) fails to deal with a matter raised in the defence,

1311.text: shall be taken to require that matter to be proved.

1312.text: General Rules About Statements Of Case

1314.text: Particulars of claim, the defence and any reply must be set out in separate consecutively numbered paragraphs and be as brief and concise as possible.

1317.text: (1) refer in his statement of case to any point of law on which his claim or defence, as the case may be, is based;

1318.text: (2) give in his statement of case the name of any witness he proposes to call; and

1319.text: (3) serve with his statement of case a copy of any document which he considers is necessary to his claim or defence, as the case may be.

1321.text: If it is necessary for the proper understanding of the statement of case to include substantial parts of a lengthy document the passages in question may be set out in a schedule rather than in the body of the statement of case .

1323.text: Only the relevant parts of those documents which are obviously of critical importance and necessary for a proper understanding of the statement of case should be attached to or served with it. The statement of case must itself refer to the fact that documents are attached to or served with it.

1325.text: If documents are to be served at the same time as a statement of case they should normally be served separately from rather than attached to the statement of case .

1327.text: Where a party relies on an oral agreement, the statement of case should set out the contractual words used and state by whom, to whom, when and where they were spoken.

1329.text: Where a party relies on an agreement by conduct, the statement of case must specify the conduct relied on and state by whom, when and where the acts constituting the conduct were done.

1331.text: A party must specifically set out the following matters in his statement of case where he wishes to rely on them in support of his case:

1332.text: (1) full and specific details of any allegation of fraud, dishonesty, malice or illegality;

1334.text: (3) details of all breaches of trust;

1335.text: (4) notice or knowledge of a fact;

1336.text: (5) details of unsoundness of mind or undue influence;

1338.text: (7) any facts relating to mitigation of loss or damage.

1340.text: Any legislative provision on which an allegation is based must be clearly identified and the basis of its application explained.

1342.text: Any principle of foreign law or foreign legislative provision on which a party’s case is based must be clearly identified and the basis of its application explained.

1344.text: An expert’s report should not be attached to the statement of case and should not be filed with the statement of case at the Registry. A party must obtain permission from the Court in order to adduce expert evidence at trial and therefore any party which serves an expert’s report without obtaining such permission does so at his own risk as to costs.

1346.text: If a statement of case exceeds 25 pages (excluding schedules), a summary, not exceeding 4 pages, must also be filed and served at the same time as the statement of case . The summary should cross-refer to the paragraph numbering of the full statement of case . The summary is to be included in the case management bundle.

1348.text: A statement of case must be signed by the individual person(s) who drafted it or bear the name of the lawyer by whom it was settled.

1350.text: A statement of case must be verified by a statement of truth, the form of which is as follows:

1351.text: [I believe][the claimant/defendant believes] that the facts stated in this claim form/these particulars of claim/this defence [and counterclaim/this reply and defence to counterclaim] are true.’

1353.text: A subsequent statement of case must not contradict or be inconsistent with an earlier one; for example a reply to a defence must not bring in a new claim. Where new matters have come to light the appropriate course is to seek the Court’s permission to amend the statement of case .

1354.text: PART 18 Amendments To Statements Of Case

1357.text: 18.1 A party may amend his statement of case at any time before it has been served on any other party.

1359.text: If his statement of case has been served, a party may amend it only:

1360.text: (1) with the written consent of all the other parties; or (2) with the permission of the Court.

1362.text: If a statement of case has been served, an application to amend it by removing, adding or substituting a party must be made in accordance with Part 20.

1363.text: Power Of Court To Disallow Amendments Made Without Permission

1364.text:
18.4   
If a party has amended his statement of case where permission of the Court was not required, the Court may disallow the amendment.

1366.text: A party may apply to the Court for an order under Rule 18.4 within 14 days of service of a copy of the amended statement of case on him.

1367.text: Amendments To Statements Of Case With The Permission Of The Court

1369.text: Where the Court gives permission for a party to amend his statement of case , it may give directions as to:

1370.text: (1) amendments to be made to any other statement of case ; and (2) service of any amended statement of case .

1372.text: The power of the Court to give permission under Rule 18.6 is subject to:

1374.text: Amendments To Statements Of Case After The End Of A Relevant Limitation Period

1377.text: (1) a party applies to amend his statement of case in one of the ways mentioned in Rule 18; and (2) a period of limitation has expired under:

1380.text: The Court may allow an amendment whose effect will be to add or substitute a new claim, but only if the new claim arises out of the same facts or substantially the same facts as a claim in respect of which the party applying for permission has already claimed a remedy in the proceedings.

1382.text: The Court may allow an amendment to correct a mistake as to the name of a party, but only where the mistake was genuine and not one which would cause reasonable doubt as to the identity of the party in question.

1384.text: The Court may allow an amendment to alter the capacity in which a party claims if the new capacity is one which that party had when the proceedings started or has since acquired.

1385.text: Applications To Amend Where The Permission Of The Court Is Required

1387.text: Questions of amendment, and consequential amendment, should wherever possible be dealt with by consent. A party should consent to a proposed amendment unless he has substantial grounds for objecting to it.

1389.text: The application may be dealt with at a hearing or, if Rule 23.68 applies, without a hearing.

1391.text: When making an application to amend a statement of case , the applicant should file with the Court:

1392.text: (1) the application notice ; (2) a copy of the statement of case with the proposed amendments; and (3) any evidence submitted in support of the application.

1394.text: Late amendments should be avoided and may be disallowed.

1397.text: Where permission to amend has been given, the applicant should within 14 days of the date of the order, or within such other period as the Court may direct, file with the Court the amended statement of case .

1399.text: A copy of the order and the amended statement of case should be served on every party to the proceedings, unless the Court orders otherwise.

1401.text: Where the Court’s permission is not required the amended statement of case must be filed with the Court and served on every party to the proceedings.

1403.text: The amended statement of case and the Court copy of it should be endorsed as follows:

1404.text: (1) where the Court’s permission was required:

1405.text: “Amended [Particulars of Claim or as may be] by Order of [The Honourable Chief Justice, the Honourable Mr. Justice X, Mr. Registrar X, Deputy Registrar X or as may be] dated……..;

1406.text: (2) Where the Court’s permission was not required:

1407.text: Amended [Particulars of Claim or as may be] under RDC [Rule 18.1 or 18.2(1)] dated…….

1409.text: The statement of case in its amended form must show the original text, unless the Court orders otherwise.

1411.text: Amendments may be shown by using footnotes or marginal notes, provided they identify precisely where and when an amendment has been made.

1413.text: However, the Court may direct that the amendments should be shown either:

1414.text: (1) by coloured amendments, either manuscript or computer generated, or

1415.text: (2) by use of a numerical code in a monochrome computer generated document.

1417.text: Where colour is used, the text to be deleted should be struck through in colour and any text replacing it should be inserted or underlined in the same colour.

1419.text: The order of colours to be used for successive amendments is: (1) red, (2) green, (3) blue and (4) brown.

1421.text: If there have been extensive amendments, it may be desirable to prepare a fresh version of the statement of case as amended. However, a copy of the statement of case showing where and when amendments have been made must also be made available.

1423.text: All amendments to any statement of case must be verified by a statement of truth unless the Court orders otherwise.

1425.text: A party applying for an amendment will usually be responsible for the costs of and arising from the amendment, but the Court will have regard to any failure of a party to consent to an amendment in accordance with Rule 18.12.

1429.text: 19.1 The Court may at any time order a party to:

1430.text: (1) clarify any matter which is in dispute in the proceedings; or (2) give additional information in relation to any such matter; whether or not the matter is contained or referred to in a statement of case .

1432.text: Rule 19.1 is subject to any rule of law to the contrary.

1434.text: Where the Court makes an order under Rule 19.1, the party against whom it is made must:

1435.text: (1) file his response; and (2) serve it on the other parties;

1436.text: within the time specified by the Court .

1437.text: Restriction On The Use Of Further Information

1439.text: The Court may direct that information provided by a party to another party (whether given voluntarily or following an order made under Rule 19.1) must not be used for any purpose except for that of the proceedings in which it is given.

1440.text: Preliminary Request For Further Information Or Clarification

1442.text: Before making an application to the Court for an order under Rule 19.1, the party seeking clarification or information should first serve on the party from whom it is sought a written request for that clarification or information (“a Request”), stating a date by which the response to the Request should be served. The date must allow the party providing clarification or further information a reasonable time to respond.

1444.text: A Request should be concise and strictly confined to matters which are reasonably necessary and proportionate to enable the first party to prepare his own case or to understand the case he has to meet.

1446.text: Requests must be made as far as possible in a single comprehensive document and not piecemeal.

1448.text:
A Request may be made by letter if the text of the Request is brief and the reply is likely to be brief;   
otherwise the Request should be made in a separate document.

1450.text: If a Request is made in a letter, the letter should, in order to distinguish it from any other that might routinely be written in the course of a case:

1451.text: (1) state that it contains a Request made under Part 19, and

1452.text: (2) deal with no matters other than the Request.

1454.text: A Request (whether made by letter or in a separate document) must:

1455.text: (1) be headed with the title and number of the claim;

1456.text: (2) in its heading state that it is a Request made under Part 19, identify the party seeking clarification or information and the party from whom it is sought and state the date on which it is made;

1457.text: (3) set out in a separate numbered paragraph each request for information or clarification;

1458.text: (4) where a Request relates to a document, identify that document and (if relevant) the paragraph or words to which it relates; and

1459.text: (5) state the date by which the party seeking clarification or information expects a response to the Request.

1461.text: A Request which is not in the form of a letter may, if convenient, be prepared in such a way that the response may be given on the same document:

1462.text: (1) to do this, the numbered paragraphs of the Request should appear on the left hand half of each sheet so that the paragraphs of the response may then appear on the right;

1463.text: (2) where a Request is prepared in this form an extra copy should be served for the use of the second party.

1465.text: Subject to Rules 9.3(1) to (4) a Request should be served by e-mail if reasonably practicable.

1468.text: A response to a Request must be in writing, dated and signed by the party providing clarification or further information or his legal representative .

1470.text: Where the Request is made in a letter:

1471.text: (1) the party providing clarification or further information may give his response in a letter or in a formal reply;

1472.text: (2) such a letter should identify itself as a response to the Request and deal with no other matters than the response.

1474.text: Unless the Request is in the format described in Rule 19.11 and the party providing clarification or further information uses the document supplied for the purpose, a response must:

1477.text: A second or supplementary response to a Request must identify itself as such in its heading.

1479.text: The party providing clarification or further information must when he serves his response on the party seeking it serve on every other party and file with the Court a copy of the Request and of his response.

1482.text: A response to a Part 19 Request must be verified by a statement of truth in the following form:

1483.text: [I believe] [the claimant/ defendant believes] that the facts stated in this response to the claimant’s/

1484.text: defendant’s Part 19 Request for further information are true”.

1487.text: If the party providing clarification or further information objects to complying with the Request or part of it or is unable to do so at all or within the time stated in the Request:

1488.text: (1) he must inform the party seeking clarification or information promptly and in any event within that time; and

1489.text: (2) he may do so in a letter or in a separate document (a formal response), but in either case he must give reasons and, where relevant, give a date by which he expects to be able to comply.

1491.text: There is no need for a party providing clarification or further information to apply to the Court if he objects to a Request or is unable to comply with it at all or within the stated time. He need only comply with Rule 19.19.

1493.text: Where a party providing clarification or further information considers that a Request can only be complied with at disproportionate expense and objects to comply for that reason he should say so in his reply and explain briefly why he has taken that view.

1496.text: An application notice for an order under Part 19 should set out or have attached to it the text of the order sought and in particular should specify the matter or matters in respect of which the clarification or information is sought.

1498.text: If a Request under Rule 19.5 for the information or clarification has not been made, the application notice should, in addition, explain why not.

1500.text: If a Request for clarification or information has been made, the application notice or the evidence in support should describe the response, if any.

1502.text: Both the party seeking clarification or information and the party from whom it is sought should consider whether evidence in support of or in opposition to the application is required.

1504.text: Where the party from whom clarification or information is sought has made no response to a Request served on him:

1505.text: (1) the party seeking the clarification or information need not serve the application notice on that party, and the Court may deal with the application without a hearing; and

1506.text: (2) sub-paragraph (1) above only applies if at least 14 days have passed since the Request was served and the time stated in it for a response has expired.

1508.text: Unless Rule 19.26 applies the application notice must be served on the second party and on all other parties to the claim.

1510.text: An order made under Part 19 must be served on all parties to the claim.

1513.text: 20.1 Any number of claimants or defendants may be joined as parties to a claim.

1515.text: Parties may be removed, added or substituted in existing proceedings either on the Court’s own initiative or on the application of either an existing party or a person who wishes to become a party.

1517.text: The application may be dealt with without a hearing where all the existing parties and the proposed new party are in agreement.

1519.text: The application to add or substitute a new party should be supported by evidence setting out the proposed new party’s interest in or connection with the claim.

1520.text: PART 20 Addition And Substitution Of Parties

1522.text: The application notice should be filed in accordance with Rule 23.2 and, unless the application is made under Rule 20.9, be served in accordance with Rule 23.25.

1523.text: I ADDITION AND SUBSTITUTION OF PARTIES

1526.text: Rules 20.6 to 20.9 apply where a party is to be added or substituted except where the case falls within Rules 20.27 to 20.29 (special provisions about changing parties after the end of a relevant limitation period ).

1528.text: The Court may order a person to be added as a new party if:

1529.text: (1) it is desirable to add the new party so that the Court can resolve all the matters in dispute in the proceedings; or

1530.text: (2) there is an issue involving the new party and an existing party which is connected to the matters in dispute in the proceedings, and it is desirable to add the new party so that the Court can resolve that issue.

1532.text: The Court may order any person to cease to be a party if it is not desirable for that person to be a party to the proceedings.

1534.text: The Court may order a new party to be substituted for an existing one if:

1535.text: (1) the existing party’s interest or liability has passed to the new party; and

1536.text: (2) it is desirable to substitute the new party so that the Court can resolve the matters in dispute in the proceedings.

1538.text: Where a claimant claims a remedy to which some other person is jointly entitled with him, all persons jointly entitled to the remedy must be parties unless the Court orders otherwise.

1539.text: Procedure For Adding And Substituting Parties

1541.text: The Court’s permission is required to remove, add or substitute a party, unless the claim form has not been served.

1543.text: An application for permission under Rule 20.11 may be made by:

1544.text: (1) an existing party; or (2) a person who wishes to become a party.

1546.text: An application for an order under Rule 20.9 (substitution of a new party where existing party’s interest or liability has passed):

1547.text:
(1) may be made without notice; and   
(2) must be supported by evidence setting out the proposed new party’s interest in, or connection with, the claim.

1549.text: An order for the removal, addition or substitution of a party must be served on:

1550.text: (1) all parties to the proceedings; and (2) any other person affected by the order.

1552.text: When the Court makes an order for the removal, addition or substitution of a party, it may give consequential directions about:

1553.text: (1) filing and serving the claim form on any new defendant ; (2) serving relevant documents on the new party; and (3) the management of the proceedings.

1556.text: Nobody may be added or substituted as a claimant unless:

1557.text: (1) he has given his consent in writing; and (2) that consent has been filed with the Court .

1559.text: If any person does not agree to be a claimant , he must be made a defendant , unless the Court orders otherwise.

1561.text: Where an application is made to the Court to add or to substitute a new party to the proceedings as claimant , the party applying must file:

1562.text: (1) the application notice ; (2) the proposed amended claim form and particulars of claim; and (3) the signed, written consent of the new claimant to be so added or substituted.

1564.text: Where the Court makes an order adding or substituting a party as claimant but the signed, written consent of the new claimant has not been filed:

1566.text: (2) the addition or substitution of the new party as claimant ;

1567.text: will not take effect until the signed, written consent of the new claimant is filed.

1569.text: Where the Court has made an order adding or substituting a new claimant , the Court may direct:

1570.text: (1) a copy of the order to be served on every party to the proceedings and any other person affected by the order;

1571.text: (2) copies of the statements of case and of documents referred to in any statement of case to be served on the new party; and

1572.text: (3) the party who made the application to file within 14 days an amended claim form and particulars of claim.

1575.text: These Rules apply to a new defendant who has been added or substituted as they apply to any other defendant .

1577.text: Where the Court has made an order adding or substituting a defendant whether on its own initiative or on an application, the Court may direct:

1578.text: (1) the claimant to file with the Court within 14 days (or as ordered) an amended claim form and particulars of claim for the Court file;

1579.text: (2) a copy of the order to be served on all parties to the proceedings and any other person affected by it;

1580.text: (3) the amended claim form and particulars of claim, forms for admitting, defending and acknowledging the claim and copies of the statements of case and any other documents referred to in any statement of case to be served on the new defendant ; and

1581.text: (4) unless the Court orders otherwise, the amended claim form and particulars of claim to be served on any other defendants .

1583.text: A new defendant does not become a party to the proceedings until the amended claim form has been served on him.

1586.text: Where the Court makes an order for the removal of a party from the proceedings:

1587.text: (1) the claimant must file with the Court an amended claim form and particulars of claim; and (2) a copy of the order must be served on every party to the proceedings and on any other person affected by the order.

1590.text: Where the interest or liability of an existing party has passed to some other person, application should be made to the Court to add or substitute that person.

1592.text: The application must be supported by evidence showing the stage the proceedings have reached and what change has occurred to cause the transfer of interest or liability.

1593.text: Special Provisions About Adding Or Substituting Parties After The End Of A Relevant Limitation Period

1595.text: Rules 20.28 to 20.29 apply to a change of parties after the end of a period of limitation under:

1597.text: (6) any other enactment which allows such an amendment, or under which such an amendment is allowed.

1599.text: The Court may add or substitute a party only if:

1600.text: (1) the relevant limitation period was current when the proceedings were started; and

1601.text: (2) the addition or substitution is necessary.

1603.text: The addition or substitution of a party is necessary only if the Court is satisfied that:

1604.text: (1) the new party is to be substituted for a party who was named in the claim form in mistake for the new party;

1605.text: (2) the claim cannot properly be carried on by or against the original party unless the new party is added or substituted as claimant or defendant ; or

1606.text: (3) the original party has died or had a bankruptcy order made against him and his interest or liability has passed to the new party.

1607.text: Special Rules About Parties In Claims For Wrongful Interference With Property

1609.text: A claimant in a claim for wrongful interference with property must, in the particulars of claim, state the name and address of every person who, to his knowledge, has or claims an interest in the property and who is not a party to the claim.

1611.text: A defendant to a claim for wrongful interference with property may apply for a direction that another person be made a party to the claim to establish whether the other person:

1612.text: (1) has a better right to the goods than the claimant ; or

1613.text: (2) has a claim which might render the defendant with double liability.

1615.text: In Rule 20.31 “double liability” means the double liability of the wrongdoer which can arise:

1616.text: (1) where one of two or more rights of action for wrongful interference is founded on a possessory title; or

1617.text: (2) where the measure of damages in an action for wrongful interference founded on a proprietary title is or includes the entire value of the goods, although the interest is one of two or more interests in the goods.

1619.text: Where the person referred to in Rule 20.31 fails to attend the hearing of the application, or comply with any directions, the Court may order that he is deprived of any claim against the defendant in respect of the goods.

1621.text: The application notice must be served on all parties and on the person referred to in Rule 20.31.

1625.text: Where more than one person has the same interest in a claim:

1626.text: (1) the claim may be begun; or

1627.text: (2) the Court may order that the claim be continued;

1628.text: by or against one or more of the persons who have the same interest as representatives of any other persons who have that interest.

1630.text: The Court may direct that a person may not act as a representative.

1632.text: Any party may apply to the Court for an order under Rule 20.36.

1634.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs any judgment or order given in a claim in which a party is acting as a representative under this rule:

1635.text:
(1) is binding on all persons represented in the claim; but   
(2) may only be enforced by or against a person who is not a party to the claim with the permission of the Court .

1637.text: This rule does not apply to a claim to which Rules 20.41 to 20.46 apply.

1638.text: Representation Of Interested Persons Who Cannot Be Ascertained Etc.

1640.text: Rules 20.41 to 20.46 apply to claims about:

1641.text: (1) the estate of a deceased person (2) property subject to a trust; or (3) the meaning of a document, including a Law.

1643.text: The Court may make an order appointing a person to represent any other person or persons in the claim where the person or persons to be represented:

1646.text: An application for an order under Rule 20.41:

1647.text:
(1) may be made by: (a) any person who seeks to be appointed under the order; or (b) any party to the claim; and   
(2) may be made at any time before or after the claim has started.

1649.text: An application notice for an order under Rule 20.41 must be served on:

1650.text:
(1) all parties to the claim, if the claim has started;   
(2) the person sought to be appointed, if that person is not the applicant or a party to the claim; and (3) any other person as directed by the Court .

1652.text: The Court’s approval is required to settle a claim in which a party is acting as a representative under Rules 20.41 to 20.46.

1654.text: The Court may approve a settlement where it is satisfied that the settlement is for the benefit of all the

1657.text: Unless the Court otherwise directs, any judgment or order given in a claim in which a party is acting as a representative under Rules 20.41 to 20.46:

1658.text:
(1) is binding on all persons represented in the claim; but   
(2) may only be enforced by or against a person who is not a party to the claim with the permission of the Court .

1659.text: Representation Of Beneficiaries By Trustees Etc.

1661.text: A claim may be brought by or against trustees, executors or administrators in that capacity without adding as parties any persons who have a beneficial interest in the trust or estate (the ‘beneficiaries’).

1663.text: Any judgment or order given or made in the claim is binding on the beneficiaries unless the Court orders otherwise in the same or other proceedings.

1666.text: Where a person who had an interest in a claim has died and that person has no personal representative (“person representing his estate”) the Court may order:

1667.text: (1) the claim to proceed in the absence of a person representing the estate of the deceased; or (2) a person to be appointed to represent the estate of the deceased.

1669.text: Where a defendant against whom a claim could have been brought has died and:

1670.text: (1) a grant of representation has been made, the claim must be brought against the persons who are the personal representatives of the deceased;

1671.text: (2) a grant of representation has not been made:

1672.text: (a) the claim must be brought against ‘the estate of’ the deceased; and

1673.text: (b) the claimant must apply to the Court for an order appointing a person to represent the estate of the deceased in the claim.

1675.text: A claim shall be treated as having been brought against ‘the estate of’ the deceased in accordance with Rule 20.50(2)(a) where:

1676.text: (1) the claim is brought against the ‘personal representatives’ of the deceased but a grant of probate or administration has not been made; or

1677.text: (2) the person against whom the claim was brought was dead when the claim was started.

1679.text: Before making an order under Rule 20.49 or 20.50(2)(b), the Court may direct notice of the application to be given to any other person with an interest in the claim.

1681.text: Where an order has been made under Rule 20.49 or 20.50(2)(b) any judgment or order made or given in the claim is binding on the estate of the deceased.

1682.text: Power To Make Judgments Binding On Non-Parties

1684.text: Rules 20.55 to 20.62 apply to any claim relating to:

1685.text: (1) the estate of a deceased person; (2) property subject to a trust; or (3) the sale of any property.

1687.text: The Court may at any time direct that notice of:

1688.text: (1) the claim; or (2) any judgment or order given in the claim; be served on any person who is not a party but who is or may be affected by it.

1691.text:
(1) may be made without notice; and   
(2) must be supported by written evidence which includes the reasons why the person to be served should be bound by the judgment in the claim.

1694.text: (1) a notice of a claim or of a judgment or order under Rule 20.55 must be:

1696.text: (2) a notice of a claim must also be accompanied by:

1697.text:
(a) a copy of the claim form:   
(b) such other statements of case, witness statements or affidavits as the Court may direct. (c) [deleted]

1699.text: If a person served with notice of a claim files an acknowledgment of service of the notice within 14 days he will become a party to the claim.

1701.text: If a person served with notice of a claim does not acknowledge service of the notice, he will be bound by any judgment given in the claim as if he were a party.

1703.text: If, after service of a notice of a claim on a person, the claim form is amended so as substantially to alter the relief claimed, the Court may direct that a judgment shall not bind that person unless a further notice, together with a copy of the amended claim form, is served on him.

1705.text: Any person served with a notice of a judgment or order under Rule 20.55:

1706.text: (1) shall be bound by the judgment or order as if he had been a party to the claim; but

1708.text: (a) within 28 days after the notice is served on him, apply to the Court to set aside or vary the judgment or order; and(b) take part in any proceedings relating to the judgment or order.

1709.text: (3) The following rules of Part 11 (acknowledgment of service ) apply:

1710.text: (a) Rule 11.7; and(b) Rule 11.8, subject to the modification that references to the defendant are to be read as references to the person served with the notice.

1712.text: A notice under Rule 20.55 is issued on the date entered on the notice by the Court .

1715.text: Rules 20.64 to 20.69 apply where a company or other incorporated body is alleged to be entitled to claim a remedy and a claim is made by one or more members of the company or body for it to be given that remedy (a ‘derivative claim’).

1717.text: The company or body for whose benefit a remedy is sought must be a defendant to the claim.

1719.text: After the claim form has been issued the claimant must apply to the Court for permission to continue the claim and may not take any other step in the proceedings except:

1723.text: An application in accordance with Rule 20.65 must be supported by written evidence.

1728.text: (3) written evidence in support of the application;

1729.text: must be served on the defendant within the period within which the claim form must be served and, in any event, at least 14 days before the Court is to deal with the application.

1731.text: If the Court gives the claimant permission to continue the claim, the Court will give directions for the management of the claim.

1733.text: The Court may order the company or body to indemnify the claimant against any liability in respect of costs incurred in the claim.

1737.text: A Group Litigation Order (‘GLO’) means an order made under Rule 20.72 to provide for the case management of claims which give rise to common or related issues of fact or law (the ‘GLO issues’).

1739.text: This Section III of Part 20 (group litigation orders) also applies where the multiple parties are defendants The Court will give such directions in such a case as are appropriate.

1742.text: The Court may make a GLO where there are or are likely to be a number of claims giving rise to the GLO issues.

1744.text: An application for a GLO must be made in accordance with Part 23, may be made at any time before or after any relevant claims have been issued and may be made either by a claimant or by a defendant .

1746.text: The following information should be included in the application notice or in written evidence filed in support of the application:

1749.text: A GLO may not be made without the consent of the Chief Justice .

1751.text: Subject to obtaining the appropriate consent referred to in Rule 20.75 the Court may make a GLO of its own initiative.

1754.text: (1) contain directions about the establishment of a register (the ‘group register’) on which the claims managed under the GLO will be entered; and

1755.text: (2) specify the GLO issues which will identify the claims to be managed as a group under the GLO.

1758.text: (1) in relation to claims which raise one or more of the GLO issues:

1759.text: (a) order their stay until further order; and

1760.text: (b) direct their entry on the group register;

1761.text: (2) direct that from a specified date claims which raise one or more of the GLO issues should be entered on the group register; and

1762.text: (3) give directions for publicising the GLO.

1765.text: A claim must be issued before it can be entered on a group register.

1767.text: An application for details of a case to be entered on a group register may be made by any party to the case.

1769.text: An order for details of the case to be entered on the group register will not be made unless the case gives rise to at least one of the GLO issues.

1771.text: The Court , if it is not satisfied that a case can be conveniently case managed with the other cases on the group register, or if it is satisfied that the entry of the case on the group register would adversely affect the case management of the other cases, may refuse to allow details of the case to be entered on the group register, or order their removal from the register if already entered, although the case gives rise to one or more of the GLO issues.

1773.text: The group register will normally be maintained by and kept at the Court but the Court may direct this to be

1774.text: done by the legal representative of one of the parties to a case entered on the register.

1776.text: Rules 6.9 to 6.19 (supply of documents from Court records) apply where the register is maintained by the Court . A party to a claim on the group register may request documents relating to any other claim on the group register in accordance with Rule 6.9 as if he were a party to those proceedings.

1778.text: Where the register is maintained by a legal representative , any person may inspect the group register during normal business hours and upon giving reasonable notice to the legal representative ; the legal representatives may charge a fee not exceeding the fee prescribed for a search at the Court office.

1781.text: Where a judgment or order is given or made in a claim on the group register in relation to one or more GLO issues:

1782.text: (1) that judgment or order is binding on the parties to all other claims that are on the group register at the time the judgment is given or the order is made unless the Court orders otherwise; and

1783.text: (2) the Court may give directions as to the extent to which that judgment or order is binding on the parties to any claim which is subsequently entered on the group register.

1785.text: Unless Rule 20.88 applies, any party who is adversely affected by a judgment or order which is binding on him may seek permission to appeal the order.

1787.text: A party to a claim which was entered on the group register after a judgment or order which is binding on him was given or made may not:

1788.text: (1) apply for the judgment or order to be set aside , varied or stayed ; or

1790.text: but may apply to the Court for an order that the judgment or order is not binding on him.

1792.text: Unless the Court orders otherwise, production of any document relating to the GLO issues by a party to a claim on the group register is production of that document to all parties to claims:

1794.text: (2) which are subsequently entered on the group register.

1799.text: (2) providing for one or more claims on the group register to proceed as test claims;

1800.text: (3) appointing the legal representative of one or more parties to be the lead legal representative for the claimants or defendants ;

1801.text: (4) specifying the details to be included in a statement of case in order to show that the criteria for entry of the claim on the group register have been met;

1802.text: (5) specifying a date after which no claim may be added to the group register unless the Court gives permission;

1803.text: (6) for the entry of any particular claim which meets one or more of the GLO issues on the group register;

1804.text: (7) for the trial of common issues; and

1805.text: (8) for the trial of individual issues.

1808.text: The Court may direct that the GLO claimants serve ‘Group Particulars of Claim’ which set out the various claims of all the claimants on the group register at the time the particulars are filed. Such particulars of claim will usually contain:

1809.text: (1) general allegations relating to all claims; and (2) a schedule containing entries relating to each individual claim specifying which of the general allegations are relied on and any specific facts relevant to the claimant .

1811.text: The directions given under Rule 20.91 should include directions as to whether the Group Particulars of Claim should be verified by a statement or statements of truth and, if so, by whom.

1813.text: The specific facts relating to each claimant on the group register may be obtained by the use of a questionnaire. Where this is proposed, the Court should be asked to approve the questionnaire. The Court may direct that the questionnaires completed by individual claimants take the place of the schedule referred to in Rule 20.91(2).

1815.text: The Court may also give directions about the form that particulars of claim relating to claims which are to be entered on the group register should take.

1818.text: A party to a claim entered on the group register may apply to the Court for the claim to be removed from the register.

1820.text: If the Court orders the claim to be removed from the group register it may give directions about the future management of the claim.

1823.text: Where a direction has been given for a claim on the group register to proceed as a test claim and that claim is settled, the Court may order that another claim on the group register be substituted as the test claim.

1825.text: Where an order is made under Rule 20.97, any order made in the test claim before the date of substitution is binding on the substituted claim unless the Court orders otherwise.

1833.text: (1) a counterclaim by a defendant against the claimant or against the claimant and some other person;

1834.text: (2) an additional claim by a defendant against any person (whether or not already a party) for contribution or indemnity or some other remedy; and

1835.text: (3) where an additional claim has been made against a person who is not already a party, any additional claim made by that person against any other person (whether or not already a party).

1838.text: (1) ‘additional claim’ means any claim other than the claim by the claimant against the defendant ; and

1839.text: (2) unless the context requires otherwise, references to a claimant or defendant include a party bringing or defending an additional claim .

1840.text: Application Of These Rules To Additional Claims

1842.text: An additional claim shall be treated as if it were a claim for the purposes of these Rules , except as provided by this Part.

1844.text: Rules 7.20 to 7.25 (time within which a claim form may be served) do not apply to additional claims .

1845.text: 21.5 Part 13 (default judgment ) applies to a counterclaim but not to other additional claims .

1847.text: Part 15 (admissions) applies to a counterclaim , but only:

1848.text: (1) Rule 15.1 (which provides that a party may admit the truth of another party’s case in writing); and (2) Rules 15.12 and 15.13 (admission by notice in writing — application for judgment ):

1849.text: PART 21 Counterclaims And Other Additional Claims

1851.text: 21.7 A defendant may make a counterclaim against a claimant by filing particulars of the counterclaim .

1853.text: A defendant may make a counterclaim against a claimant :

1854.text: (1) without the Court’s permission if he files it with his defence. The defence and counterclaim should normally form one document, with the counterclaim following on from the defence; or

1855.text: (2) at any other time with the Court’s permission.

1857.text: Part 11 (acknowledgment of service ) does not apply to a claimant who wishes to defend a counterclaim .

1859.text: Where a claimant serves a reply and a defence to counterclaim , the reply and the defence to counterclaim should normally form one document, with the defence to counterclaim following on from the reply.

1860.text: Counterclaim Against A Person Other Than The Claimant

1862.text: A defendant who wishes to counterclaim against a person who is not already a party must apply to the Court for an order that that person be added as an additional party.

1864.text: An application for an order under Rule 21.11 may be made without notice unless the Court directs otherwise.

1866.text: Where the Court makes an order under Rule 21.11, it will give directions as to the management of the case.

1867.text: Defendant’S Additional Claim For Contribution Or Indemnity From Another Party

1869.text: A defendant who has filed an acknowledgment of service or a defence may make an additional claim for contribution or indemnity against a person who is already a party to the proceedings by:

1870.text: (1) filing a notice containing a statement of the nature and grounds of his additional claim ; and

1872.text: (2) serving the notice on that party.

1874.text: A defendant may file and serve a notice under Rule 21.14:

1875.text: (1) without the Court’s permission, if he files and serves it: (a) with his defence; or (b) if his additional claim for contribution or indemnity is against a party added to the claim later, within 28 days after that party files his defence; or

1876.text: (2) at any other time with the Court’s permission.

1877.text: Procedure For Making Any Other Additional Claim

1879.text: Rules 21.17 to 21.24 apply to any additional claim except:

1880.text: (1) a counterclaim only against an existing party; and (2) a claim for contribution or indemnity made in accordance with Rule 21.14.

1882.text: An additional claim is made when the Court issues the appropriate claim form.

1884.text: A defendant may make an additional claim :

1885.text: (1) without the Court’s permission if the additional claim is issued before or at the same time as he files his defence; and

1886.text: (2) at any other time with the Court’s permission.

1888.text: Particulars of an additional claim must be contained in or served with the additional claim .

1890.text: An application for permission to make an additional claim may be made without notice, unless the Court directs otherwise.

1892.text: Where an application is made for permission to make an additional claim , the application notice should be filed together with a copy of the proposed additional claim .

1894.text: An application for permission to make an additional claim must be supported by evidence stating:

1897.text: Where delay has been a factor contributing to the need to apply for permission to make an additional claim an explanation of the delay should be given in evidence.

1899.text: Where possible the applicant should provide a timetable of the proceedings to date.

1902.text: Where an additional claim may be made without the Court’s permission, any claim form must:

1903.text: (1) in the case of a counterclaim against an additional party only, be served on every other party when a copy of the defence is served;

1904.text: (2) in the case of any other additional claim , be served on the person against whom it is made within 14 days after the date on which the additional claim is issued by the Court .

1906.text: Rule 21.25 does not apply to a claim for contribution or indemnity made in accordance with Rule 21.14.

1908.text: Where the Court gives permission to make an additional claim , it will at the same time give directions as to its service .

1911.text: The contents of an additional claim should be verified by a statement of truth.

1912.text: Matters Relevant To Question Of Whether An Additional Claim Should Be Separate From The Claim

1914.text: Rule 21.30 applies where the Court is considering whether to:

1917.text: The matters to which the Court may have regard include:

1918.text: (1) the connection between the additional claim and the claim made by the claimant against the defendant ;

1919.text: (2) whether the additional claimant is seeking substantially the same remedy which some other party is claiming from him; and

1920.text: (3) whether the additional claimant wants the Court to decide any question connected with the subject matter of the proceedings:

1921.text: (a) not only between existing parties but also between existing parties and a person not already a party; or

1922.text: (b) against an existing party not only in a capacity in which he is already a party but also in some further capacity.

1923.text: Effect Of Service Of An Additional Claim

1925.text: A person on whom an additional claim is served becomes a party to the proceedings if he is not a party already.

1927.text: When an additional claim is served on an existing party for the purpose of requiring the Court to decide a question against that party in a further capacity, that party also becomes a party in the further capacity specified in the additional claim .

1928.text: Special Provisions Relating To Default Judgment On An Additional Claim Other Than A Counterclaim Or A Contribution Or Indemnity Notice

1932.text:
(a) a counterclaim ; or   
(b) a claim by a defendant for contribution or indemnity against another defendant under Rule 21.14; and

1933.text: (2) the party against whom an additional claim is made fails to file an acknowledgment of service or defence in respect of the additional claim .

1935.text: The party against whom the additional claim is made is deemed to admit the additional claim , and is bound by any judgment or decision in the proceedings in so far as it is relevant to any matter arising in the additional claim .

1937.text: Subject to Rule 21.36, if default judgment under Part 13 is given against the additional defendant , the additional claimant may obtain judgment in respect of the additional claim by filing a request in Form P13/01 or Form P13/02.

1939.text: An additional claimant may not enter judgment under Rule 21.35 without the Court’s permission if:

1940.text: (1) he has not satisfied the default judgment which has been given against him; or

1941.text: (2) he wishes to obtain judgment for any remedy other than a contribution or indemnity .

1943.text: An application for the Court’s permission under Rule 21.36 may be made without notice unless the Court directs otherwise.

1945.text: The Court may at any time set aside or vary a judgment entered under Rule 21.35.

1946.text: Procedural Steps On Service Of An Additional Claim Form On A Non-Party
