7847.text: (1) any condition applying to the account that a receipt for money deposited in the account must be produced before any money is withdrawn;

7848.text: (2) any condition that notice is required before any money or share is withdrawn;

7849.text: (3) any condition that a personal application must be made before any money or share is withdrawn;

7850.text: PART 47 Attachment Of Future Assets And Earnings

7851.text: (4) any condition that a deposit book or share-account book must be produced before any money or share is withdrawn; or

7853.text: Application For Third Party Debt Order

7854.text: 47.6 An application for a third party debt order may be made without notice.

7855.text: 47.7 An application for a third party debt order must be made by filing an application notice in Form P47/01.

7857.text: The application notice must contain the following information:

7859.text: (5) the name and address of the third party;

7860.text: (6) if the third party is a bank : (a) its name and the address of the branch at which the judgment debtor’s account is believed to be held; and (b) the account number;

7861.text: or, if the judgment creditor does not know all or part of this information, that fact;

7862.text: (7) confirmation that to the best of the judgment creditor’s knowledge or belief the third party:

7863.text: (a) is within the DIFC ; and (b) owes money to or holds money to the credit of the judgment debtor ;

7864.text: (8) if the judgment creditor knows or believes that any person other than the judgment debtor has any claim to the money owed by the third party:

7865.text: (a) his name and (if known) his address; and (b) such information as is known to the judgment creditor about his claim; (9) details of any other applications for third party debt orders issued by the judgment creditor in

7866.text: respect of the same judgment debt; and

7867.text: (10) the sources or grounds of the judgment creditor’s knowledge or belief of the matters referred to in (7), (8) and (9).

7869.text: The application notice must be verified by a statement of truth.

7871.text: The Court will not grant speculative applications for third party debt orders, and will only make an interim third party debt order against a bank if the judgment creditor’s application notice contains evidence to substantiate his belief that the judgment debtor has an account with the bank in question.

7874.text: An application for a third party debt order will initially be dealt with by the Registrar without a hearing.

7876.text: The Registrar may make an interim third party debt order:

7877.text: (1) fixing a hearing to consider whether to make a final third party debt order; and (2) directing that until that hearing the third party must not make any payment which reduces the amount he owes the judgment debtor to less than the amount specified in the order.

7879.text: An interim third party debt order will specify the amount of money which the third party must retain, which will be the total of:

7880.text: (1) the amount of money remaining due to the judgment creditor under the judgment or order ; and (2) an amount for the judgment creditor’s fixed costs of the application, as specified in Part 39.

7882.text: An interim third party debt order becomes binding on a third party when it is served on him.

7884.text: The date of the hearing to consider the application shall be not less than 28 days after the interim third party debt order is made.

7887.text: Copies of an interim third party debt order, the application notice and any documents filed in support of it must be served by the judgment creditor :

7888.text:
(1) on the third party, not less than 21 days before the date fixed for the hearing; and   
(2) on the judgment debtor not less than: (a) 7 days after a copy has been served on the third party; and (b) 7 days before the date fixed for the hearing.

7891.text: (1) file a certificate of service not less than 2 days before the hearing; or (2) produce a certificate of service at the hearing.

7892.text: Obligations Of Third Parties Served With An Interim Order

7894.text: A bank served with an interim third party debt order must carry out a search to identify all accounts held with it by the judgment debtor .

7896.text: The bank must disclose to the Court and the creditor within 7 days of being served with the order, in respect of each account held by the judgment debtor :

7899.text: A bank served with an interim third party debt order is only required, unless the order states otherwise:

7900.text: (1) to retain money in accounts held solely by the judgment debtor (or, if there are joint judgment

7901.text: debtors , accounts held jointly by them or solely by either or any of them); and

7902.text: (2) to search for and disclose information about such accounts.

7904.text: The bank is not required, for example, to retain money in, or disclose information about:</ p>

7905.text: (1) accounts in the joint names of the judgment debtor and another person; or (2) if the interim order has been made against a firm, accounts in the names of individual members of that firm.

7908.text: (1) the judgment debtor does not hold an account with the bank ; or

7909.text: (2) the bank is unable to comply with the order for any other reason (for example, because it has more than one account holder whose details match the information contained in the order, and cannot identify which account the order applies to);

7910.text: the bank must inform the Court and the judgment creditor of that fact within 7 days of being served with the order.

7912.text: Any third party other than a bank served with an interim third party debt order must notify the Court and the judgment creditor in writing within 7 days of being served with the order, if he claims:

7913.text: (1) not to owe any money to the judgment debtor ; or (2) to owe less than the amount specified in the order.

7914.text: Attachment Of Debts Owed By A Partnership

7916.text: Rules 47.25 and 47.26 relate to debts due or accruing due to a judgment creditor from a partnership.

7918.text: An interim third party debt order under Rule 47.12 relating to such debts must be served on:

7921.text: Where an order made under Rule 47.12 requires a partnership to appear before the Court , it will be sufficient for a partner to appear before the Court .

7925.text: (1) a judgment debtor is an individual;

7926.text: (2) he is prevented from withdrawing money from his account with a bank as a result of an interim third party debt order; and

7927.text: (3) he or his family is suffering hardship in meeting ordinary living expenses as a result;

7928.text: the Court may, on an application by the judgment debtor , make an order permitting the bank to make a payment or payments out of the account (‘a hardship payment order’).

7930.text: An application notice seeking a hardship payment order must:

7931.text: (1) include detailed evidence explaining why the judgment debtor needs a payment of the amount requested; and

7932.text: (2) be verified by a statement of truth.

7934.text: The evidence filed by a judgment debtor in support of an application for a hardship payment order should include documentary evidence, for example (if appropriate) bank statements, wage slips and mortgage statements, to prove his financial position and need for the payment.

7936.text: The Court will treat an application for a hardship payment order as being made:

7937.text: (1) in the proceedings in which the interim third party debt order was made; and

7940.text: Unless the Court orders otherwise, the application notice :

7941.text: (1) must be served on the judgment creditor at least 2 days before the hearing; but

7942.text: (2) does not need to be served on the third party.

7944.text: In cases of exceptional urgency the judgment debtor may apply for a hardship payment order without notice to the judgment creditor and the Court will decide whether to:

7945.text: (1) deal with the application without it being served on the judgment creditor ; or (2) direct it to be served.

7947.text: If the Court decides to deal with the application without it being served on the judgment creditor , where possible it will normally:

7948.text: (1) direct that the judgment creditor be informed of the application; and (2) give him the opportunity to make representations; by telephone, fax or other appropriate method of communication.

7951.text: (1) permit the third party to make one or more payments out of the account; and (2) specify to whom the payments may be made.

7954.text: If the judgment debtor or the third party objects to the Court making a final third party debt order, he must file and serve written evidence stating the grounds for his objections.

7956.text: If the judgment debtor or the third party knows or believes that a person other than the judgment debtor has any claim to the money specified in the interim order, he must file and serve written evidence stating his knowledge of that matter.

7959.text: (1) the third party has given notice under Rule 47.23 that he does not owe any money to the judgment debtor , or that the amount which he owes is less than the amount specified in the interim order; and

7960.text: (2) the judgment creditor wishes to dispute this;

7961.text: the judgment creditor must file and serve written evidence setting out the grounds on which he disputes the third party’s case.

7963.text: Written evidence under Rules 47.35, 47.36 and 47.37 must be filed and served on each other party as soon as possible, and in any event not less than 3 days before the hearing.

7965.text: If the judgment creditor is notified that some person other than the judgment debtor may have a claim to the money specified in the interim order, he must serve on that person notice of the application and the hearing.

7967.text: At the hearing the Court may:

7968.text: (1) make a final third party debt order;

7969.text: (2) discharge the interim third party debt order and dismiss the application;

7970.text: (3) decide any issues in dispute between the parties, or between any of the parties and any other person who has a claim to the money specified in the interim order; or

7971.text: (4) direct a trial of any such issues, and if necessary give directions.

7972.text: Effect Of Final Third Party Order

7974.text: A final third party debt order shall be enforceable as an order to pay money.

7977.text: (1) the third party pays money to the judgment creditor in compliance with a third party debt order; or

7978.text: (2) the order is enforced against him;

7979.text: the third party shall, to the extent of the amount paid by him or realised by enforcement against him, be discharged from his debt to the judgment debtor .

7981.text: Rule 47.42 applies even if the third party debt order, or the original judgment or order against the judgment debtor , is later set aside .

7984.text: If money is standing to the credit of the judgment debtor in Court:

7985.text: (1) the judgment creditor may not apply for a third party debt order in respect of that money; but (2) he may apply for an order that the money in Court , or so much of it as is sufficient to satisfy the judgment or order and the costs of the application, be paid to him.

7987.text: An application notice seeking an order under Rule 47.44(2) must be served on:

7991.text: If an application notice has been issued under Rule 47.45, the money in Court must not be paid out until the application has been disposed of.

7994.text: If the judgment creditor is awarded costs on an application for an order under Rule 47.3 or 47.44:

7995.text: (1) he shall, unless the Court otherwise directs, retain those costs out of the money recovered by him under the order; and

7996.text: (2) the costs shall be considered to be paid first out of the money he recovers, in priority to the judgment debt.

8000.text: A judgment creditor who desires to apply for an attachment of earnings order shall file his application certifying the amount of money remaining due under the judgment or order and that the whole or part of any instalment due remains unpaid.

8003.text: Notice of the application together with a form of reply in the appropriate form, shall be served on the judgment debtor in the manner set out in Rule 9.2.

8005.text: The judgment debtor shall, within 8 days after service on him of the documents mentioned in Rule 47.49, file a reply in the form provided.

8007.text: Nothing in Rule 47.50 shall require a defendant to file a reply if, within the period of time mentioned in that Rule, he pays to the judgment creditor the money remaining due under the judgment or order and, where such payment is made, the judgment creditor shall so inform the Court .

8009.text: On receipt of a reply the Court shall send a copy to the applicant.

8012.text: The Court may, at any stage of the proceedings, send to any person appearing to have the judgment debtor in his employment a notice requesting him to give to the Court , within such period as may be specified in the notice, a statement of the judgment debtor’s earnings and anticipated earnings with such particulars as may be so specified.

8015.text: On receipt of the judgment debtor’s reply, the Court may, if it has sufficient information to do so, make an attachment of earnings order and a copy of the order shall be sent to the parties and to the judgment debtor’s employer.

8017.text: Where an order is made under Rule 47.54, the judgment creditor or the judgment debtor may, within 14 days of service of the order on him and giving his reasons, apply on notice for the order to be re-considered and the Court shall fix a day for the hearing of the application and give to the judgment creditor and the judgment debtor not less than 2 days’ notice of the day so fixed.

8019.text: On hearing an application under Rule 47.55, the Court may confirm the order or set it aside and make such new order as he thinks fit and the order so made shall be entered in the records of the Court .

8021.text: Where an order is not made under Rule 47.54, the Court will fix a day for the hearing of the application and give to the judgment creditor and the judgment debtor not less than 8 days’ notice of the day so fixed.

8023.text: If the judgment creditor does not appear at the hearing of the application under Rule 47.57 but:

8024.text: (1) the Court has received a witness statement or affidavit of evidence from him; or

8025.text: (2) the judgment creditor requests the Court in writing to proceed in his absence, the Court may proceed to hear the application and to make an order thereon.

8027.text: An attachment of earnings order may be made to secure the payment of a judgment debt if the debt is:

8028.text: (1) of not less than $\mathsf { U S$ 100 }$ ; or

8029.text: (2) for the amount remaining payable under a judgment for a sum of not less than $\mathsf { U S$ 100 }$ .

8032.text: If the judgment debtor has failed to comply with Rule 47.50 or to make payment to the judgment creditor , the Court may issue an order which shall:

8033.text: (1) be indorsed with or incorporate a notice warning the judgment debtor of the consequences of disobedience to the order;

8034.text: (2) be served on the judgment debtor personally; and

8035.text: (3) direct that any payments made thereafter shall be paid into the Court and not direct to the judgment creditor .

8037.text: If the person served with an order made pursuant to Rule 47.60 fails to obey it or to file a statement of his means or to make payment, the Court will issue a notice calling on that person to show good reason why he should not be committed for contempt of Court and any such notice shall be served on the judgment debtor personally not less than 5 days before the hearing.

8039.text: Any failure to comply with an order made pursuant to Rule 47.60 is punishable by committal for contempt of Court .

8042.text: If the judgment debtor fails to attend at an adjourned hearing of an application for an attachment of earnings order and a committal order is made, the Judge may direct that the committal order shall be suspended so long as the judgment debtor attends at the time and place specified in the committal order.

8044.text: Where a committal order is suspended under Rule 47.63 and the judgment debtor fails to attend at the time and place specified under Rule 47.63, a certificate to that effect given by the Court shall be sufficient

8045.text: authority for the issue of a warrant of committal.

8048.text: Where costs are allowed to the judgment creditor on an application for an attachment of earnings order, there may be allowed:

8049.text: (1) a charge of a legal representative for attending the hearing and, if the Court so directs, for serving the application; and

8050.text: (2) the Court fee on the issue of the application.

8052.text: For the purpose of Rule 47.65(1), a legal representative who has prepared on behalf of the judgment creditor a witness statement or affidavit or request under Rule 47.58 shall be treated as having attended the hearing.

8054.text: The costs may be fixed and allowed without detailed assessment under Part 40.

8057.text: An attachment of earnings order shall contain such of the following particulars relating to the debtor as are known to the Court , namely:

8060.text: An attachment of earnings order and any order varying or discharging such an order shall be served on the judgment debtor and on the person to whom the order is directed, and Part 9 and Rules 36.25, 36.26 and 36.27 shall apply with the further modification that where the order is directed to a corporation which has requested the Court that any communication relating to the judgment debtor or to the class of persons to whom he belongs shall be directed to the corporation at a particular address, service may, if the Court thinks fit, be effected on the corporation at that address.

8061.text: Application To Determine Whether Particular Payments Are Earnings

8063.text: Where an attachment of earnings order is in force, the Court shall, on the application of the employer, the judgment debtor or the person to whom payment under the relevant adjudication is required to be made, determine whether payments to the debtor of a particular class or description specified by the application are earnings for the purposes of the order; and the employer shall be entitled to give effect to any determination for the time being in force.

8065.text: An application to the Court to determine whether payments to the debtor of a particular class or description are earnings for the purpose of an attachment of earnings order may be made to the Court in writing and the Court shall thereupon fix a date and time for the hearing of the application and give notice thereof to the employer, the judgment debtor and the person to whom payment under the relevant adjudication is required to be made.

8066.text: Notice Of Order Ceasing To Have Effect

8068.text: An attachment of earnings order made to secure the payment of a judgment debt shall cease to have effect on the making of an order for committal or the issue of a warrant of committal for the enforcement of the debt.

8070.text: Where an attachment of earnings order ceases to have effect as set out in Rule 47.72 above, the Court shall give notice to the person to whom the order was directed.

8071.text: Variation And Discharge By Court On Own Initiative

8073.text: The Court may make an order discharging or varying an attachment of earnings order.

8075.text: Subject to Rule 47.82, the Court may make an order under Rule 47.74 of its own motion in the circumstances mentioned in Rules 47.76 to 47.81.

8077.text: Where it appears to the Court that a person served with an attachment of earnings order directed to him does not employ the judgment debtor , the Court may discharge the order.

8079.text: Where an attachment of earnings order which has lapsed as a result of the judgment debtor ceasing to be in the employment of the person to whom the order is directed is again directed to a person who appears to the Court to have the judgment debtor in his employment, the Court may make such consequential variations in the order as it thinks fit.

8081.text: Where, after making an attachment of earnings order, the Court makes or is notified of the making of another such order in respect of the same judgment debtor which is not to secure the payment of a judgment debt, the Court may discharge or vary the first-mentioned order in the following order of priority:

8082.text: (1) dealing first with any order which is not made to secure the payment of a judgment debt; and

8083.text: (2) dealing thereafter with any order which is made to secure the payment of a judgment debt as if the earnings to which it relates were the residue of the debtor’s earnings after the making of any deduction to comply with an order having priority by virtue of (1); and

8084.text: (3) if there are two or more orders to which (2) applies:

8085.text: (a) dealing with the orders according to the respective dates on which they were made, disregarding any later order until an earlier one has been dealt with;

8086.text: (b) dealing with any later order as if the earnings to which it relates were the residue of the debtor’s earnings after the making of any deduction to comply with any earlier order.

8088.text: On making a consolidated attachment of earnings order the Court may discharge any earlier attachment of earnings order made to secure the payment of a judgment debt by the same debtor.

8090.text: Where it appears to the Court that a bankruptcy order has been made against a person in respect of whom an attachment of earnings order is in force to secure the payment of a judgment debt, the Court may discharge the attachment of earnings order.

8092.text: Where an attachment of earnings order has been made to secure the payment of a judgment debt and the Court grants permission to issue execution for the recovery of the debt, the Court may discharge the order.

8094.text: Before varying or discharging an attachment of earnings order of its own motion under Rules 47.76 to 47.81, the Court shall, unless it thinks it unnecessary in the circumstances to do so, give the judgment debtor and the person on whose application the order was made an opportunity of being heard on the question of whether the order should be varied or discharged, and for that purpose the Court Officer may give them notice of a date, time and place at which the question will be considered.

8095.text: Cases In Which Consolidated Order May Be Made

8097.text: Subject to the provisions of Rules 47.84 to 47.94, the Court may make a consolidated attachment order where:

8098.text: (1) two or more attachment of earnings orders are in force to secure the payment of judgment debts by the same debtor; or

8099.text: (2) on an application for an attachment of earnings order to secure the payment of a judgment debt, or for a consolidated attachment order to secure the payment of two or more judgment debts, it appears to the Court that an attachment of earnings order is already in force to secure the payment of a judgment debt by the same debtor.

8102.text: An application for a consolidated attachment order may be made:

8103.text:
(1) by the judgment debtor in respect of whom the order is sought; or   
(2) by any person who has obtained or is entitled to apply for an attachment of earnings order to secure the payment of a judgment debt by that debtor.

8105.text: An application under Rule 47.84 may be made in the proceedings in which any attachment of earnings order is in force.

8107.text: An application under Rule 47.84(2) shall certify the amount of money remaining due under the judgment or order and that the whole or part of any instalment due remains unpaid.

8109.text: Where an application for a consolidated attachment of earnings order is made, the Court will:

8110.text: (1) notify any party who may be affected by the application of its terms; and

8111.text: (2) require him to notify the Court in writing, within 14 days of service of notification upon him, giving his reasons for any objection he may have to the granting of the application.

8113.text: If notice of any objection is not given within the time stated, the Court will make a consolidated attachment of earnings order.

8115.text: If any party objects to the making of a consolidated attachment of earnings order, the Court may grant the application after considering the objection made and the reasons given.

8117.text: In Rule 47.87, a party affected by the application means:

8118.text: (1) where the application is made by the judgment debtor , the creditor in the proceedings in which the application is made and any other creditor who has obtained an attachment of earnings order which is in force to secure the payment of a judgment debt by the judgment debtor ;

8119.text: (2) where the application is made by the judgment creditor , the judgment debtor and every person who, to the knowledge of the applicant, has obtained an attachment of earnings order which is in force to secure the payment of a judgment debt by the debtor.

8121.text: A person to whom two or more attachment of earnings orders are directed to secure the payment of judgment debts by the same judgment debtor may request the Court in writing to make a consolidated attachment order to secure the payment of those debts, and on receipt of such a request Rules 47.87 to 47.90 will apply, with the necessary modifications, as if the request were an application by the judgment creditor .

8122.text: Making Of A Consolidated Order By The Court On Its Own Initiative

8124.text: Where an application is made for an attachment of earnings order to secure the payment of a judgment debt by a judgment debtor in respect of whom an attachment of earnings order is already in force to secure the payment of another judgment debt and no application is made for a consolidated attachment order, the Court may make such an order on its own initiative after giving all persons concerned an opportunity of submitting written objections.

8127.text: Where a consolidated attachment order is in force to secure the payment of two or more judgment debts, any creditor to whom another judgment debt is owed by the same judgment debtor may apply to the Court for it to be extended so as to secure the payment of that debt as well as the first-mentioned debts and, if the application is granted, the Court may either vary the order accordingly or may discharge it and make a new consolidated attachment order to secure payment of all the aforesaid judgment debts.

8129.text: An application under Rule 47.93 shall be treated for the purposes of Rules 47.84 to 47.91 as an application for a consolidated attachment order.

8132.text: If the Court receives payments in compliance with a consolidated attachment order it will, after deducting such Court fees, if any, in respect of proceedings for or arising out of the order as are deductible from those payments, deal with the sums paid as if they had been paid by the judgment debtor to satisfy the relevant adjudications in proportion to the amounts payable thereunder, and for that purpose dividends may from

8133.text: time to time be declared and distributed among the creditors entitled thereto.

8139.text: (1) ‘enforcement officer’ means the Court Bailiff or an individual who is authorised to act as such by the Chief Justice or a person acting on his behalf; and

8141.text: (a) in relation to an order for execution against assets which is directed to a single enforcement officer , that officer;

8142.text: (b) in relation to an order for execution against assets which is directed to two or more enforcement officers , the officer to whom the order is allocated;

8143.text: (3) ‘writ of execution’ includes a writ of execution against assets, a writ of possession, or a writ of delivery, a writ of sequestration and any further writ in aid of any of the aforementioned writs.

8146.text: When first executing an order for execution against assets, the relevant enforcement officer shall deliver to the debtor or leave at each place where execution is levied, a notice in Form P48/01 informing the debtor of the execution.

8147.text: Enforcement Of Judgment For Delivery Of Goods

8149.text: Subject to the provisions of this Part, a judgment or order for the delivery of any goods which does not give a person against whom the judgment is given or order made, the alternative of paying the assessed value of the goods may be enforced by one or more of the following means:

8150.text: (1) an order of delivery to recover the goods without alternative provision for recovery of the assessed

8151.text: value thereof (hereafter in this Part referred to as an ‘order of specific delivery’);

8152.text: (2) in a case in which Rule 48.8 applies:

8153.text: (a) an order of committal; or (b) a writ of sequestration.

8155.text: Subject to the provisions of this Part, a judgment or order for the delivery of any goods or payment of their assessed value may be enforced by one or more of the following means:

8156.text: (1) an order of delivery to recover the goods or their assessed value;

8157.text: (2) by order of the Court , an order of specific delivery;

8158.text: (3) in a case in which Rule 48.8 applies, a writ of sequestration.

8160.text: An application for an order under Rule 48.4(2) shall be made in accordance with Part 23, which must be served on the defendant against whom the judgment or order sought to be enforced was given or made.

8162.text: A writ of specific delivery, and a writ of delivery to recover any goods or their assessed value, may include provision for enforcing the payment of any money adjudged or ordered to be paid by the judgment or order which is to be enforced by the writ.

8164.text: A judgment or order for the payment of the assessed value of any goods may be enforced by the same means as any other judgment or order for the payment of money .

8165.text: Enforcement Of Judgment To Do Or Abstain From Doing Any Act

8168.text: (1) a person required by a judgment or order to do an act within a time specified in the judgment or order refuses or neglects to do it within that time or, as the case may be, within that time as extended or abridged under a Court order or Rule 2.19; or

8169.text: (2) a person disobeys a judgment or order requiring him to abstain from doing an act;

8170.text: then, subject to the provisions of these Rules, the judgment or order may be enforced by one or more of the following means:

8171.text: (a) with the permission of the Court , a writ of sequestration against the property of that person;

8172.text: (b) where that person is a body corporate, with the permission of the Court , a writ of sequestration against the property of any director or other officer of the body;

8173.text: (c) an order of committal against that person or, where that person is a body corporate, against any such officer.

8175.text: Where a judgment or order requires a person to do an act within a specified time and an order is subsequently made under Rule 48.11 requiring the act to be done within some other time, references in Rule 48.8 to a judgment or order shall be construed as references to the order made under Rule 48.11.

8177.text: Where under any judgment or order requiring the delivery of any goods the person liable to execution has the alternative of paying the assessed value of the goods, the judgment or order shall not be enforceable by order of committal under Rule 48.8, but the Court may, on the application of the person entitled to enforce the judgment or order , make an order requiring the first mentioned person to deliver the goods to the applicant within a time specified in the order, and that order may be so enforced.

8178.text: Judgment Or Order Requiring Act To Be Done: Order Fixing Time For Doing It

8180.text: Notwithstanding that a judgment or order requiring a person to do an act specifies a time within which the act is to be done, the Court shall have power to make an order requiring the act to be done within another time, being such time after service of that order, or such other time, as may be specified therein.

8182.text: Where a judgment or order requiring a person to do an act does not specify a time within which the act is to be done, the Court shall have power subsequently to make an order requiring the act to be done within such time after service of that order, or such other time, as may be specified therein.

8184.text: An application for an order under Rule 48.11 or Rule 48.12 must be made in accordance with Part 23 and the application notice must be served on the person required to do the act in question.

8185.text: Service Of Copy Of Judgment Or Order Prerequisite To Enforcement Under Rule 48.8

8187.text: In Rules 48.15 to 48.20, references to an order shall be construed as including references to a judgment.

8189.text: Subject to Rules 48.19 and 48.20, an order shall not be enforced under Rule 48.8 unless:

8190.text: (1) a copy of the order has been served personally on the person required to do or abstain from doing the act in question; and

8191.text: (2) in the case of an order requiring a person to do an act, the copy has been so served before the expiration of the time within which he was required to do the act.

8193.text: Subject as aforesaid, an order requiring a body corporate to do or abstain from doing an act shall not be enforced as mentioned in Rule 48.8 unless:

8194.text: (1) a copy of the order has also been served personally on the officer against whose property permission is sought to issue a writ of sequestration or against whom an order of committal is sought; and

8195.text: (2) in the case of an order requiring the body corporate to do an act, the copy has been so served before the expiration of the time within which the body was required to do the act.

8197.text: There must be prominently displayed on the front of the copy of an order served under Rule 48.15 or 48.16 a warning to the person on whom the copy is served that disobedience to the order would be a contempt of Court punishable by a fine, or (in the case of an order requiring a body corporate to do or abstain from doing an act) punishable by sequestration of the assets of the body corporate and by a fine of any individual responsible.

8199.text: With the copy of an order required to be served under Rule 48.15 or 48.16, being an order requiring a person to do an act, there must also be served a copy of any order or agreement under Rule 2.19 extending or abridging the time for doing the act and, where the first-mentioned order was made under Rule 48.11 or Rule 48.12, a copy of the previous order requiring the act to be done.

8201.text: An order requiring a person to abstain from doing an act may be enforced under Rule 48.8 notwithstanding that service of a copy of the order has not been effected in accordance with Rule 48.15 or 48.16 if the Court is satisfied that pending such service , the person against whom or against whose property is sought to enforce the order has had notice thereof either:

8202.text: (1) by being present when the order was made; or (2) by being notified of the terms of the order, whether by telephone, telegram or otherwise.

8204.text: The Court may dispense with service of a copy of an order under Rules 48.15 to 48.18 if it thinks it just to do so.

8205.text: Court May Order Act To Be Done At The Expense Of The Disobedient Party

8207.text: If a mandatory order, an injunction or a judgment or order for the specific performance of a contract is not complied with, then the Court may direct that the act required to be done may, so far as practicable, be done by the party by whom the order or judgment was obtained or some other person appointed by the Court , at the cost of the disobedient party, and upon the act being done, the expenses incurred may be ascertained in such manner as the Court may direct and execution may issue against the disobedient party for the amount so ascertained and for costs.

8208.text: Matters Occurring After Judgment: Stay Of Execution

8210.text: A party against whom a judgment has been given or an order made may apply to the Court for a stay of execution of the judgment or order or other relief on the ground of matters which have occurred since the date of the judgment or order , and the Court may by order grant such relief, and on such terms, as it thinks just.

8213.text: An order for execution against assets must be in Form P48/02.

8215.text: An order of delivery must be in Form P48/03.

8217.text: A writ of possession must be in Form P48/04.

8219.text: A writ of sequestration must be in Form P48/05.

8221.text: Unless the Court orders otherwise, an order of execution to enforce a judgment or order must not be executed on a day that is not a business day .

8222.text: No Enforcement 6 Years After Order Made

8224.text: The Court shall not make an order to enforce a judgment or order after 6 years have elapsed from the date when the judgment or order was made.

8225.text: When Permission To Issue Any Writ Of Execution Is Necessary

8227.text: An order of execution to enforce a judgment or order may not issue without the permission of the Court in the following cases:

8228.text: (1) where any change has taken place, whether by death or otherwise, in the parties entitled or liable to execution under the judgment or order ;

8229.text: (2) where the judgment or order is against the assets of a deceased person coming to the hands of his executors or administrators after the date of the judgment or order , and it is sought to issue execution against such assets;

8230.text: (3) where under the judgment or order , any person is entitled to a remedy subject to the fulfilment of any condition which it is alleged has been fulfilled;

8231.text: (4) where any goods sought to be seized under an order of execution are in the hands of a receiver appointed by the Court or a sequestrator.

8233.text: Rule 48.29 is without prejudice to any enactment or Rule by virtue of which a person is required to obtain the permission of the Court for the issue of a writ of execution or to proceed to execution on or otherwise to the enforcement of a judgment or order .

8235.text: Where the Court grants permission, whether under Rule 48.29 or otherwise, for the issue of a writ of execution and the writ is not issued within one year after the date of the order granting such permission, the order shall cease to have effect, without prejudice, however, to the making of a fresh order.

8236.text: Permission Required For Issue Of Writ In Aid Of Other Writ

8238.text: A writ of execution in aid of any other writ of execution shall not issue without the permission of the Court .

8239.text: Application For Permission To Issue Writ

8241.text: An application for permission to issue a writ of execution may be made in accordance with Part 23 but the application notice need not be served on the respondent unless the Court directs.

8243.text: Such an application must be supported by a witness statement or affidavit :

8244.text: (1) identifying the judgment or order to which the application relates and, if the judgment or order is for the payment of money, stating the amount originally due and the amount due at the date the

8246.text: (2) stating, where the case falls within Rule 48.29(1), the change which has taken place in the parties entitled or liable to execution since the date of the judgment or order ;

8247.text: (3) stating, where the case falls within Rule 48.29(2) or 48.29(3), that a demand to satisfy the judgment or order was made on the person liable to satisfy it, and that he has refused or failed to do so;

8248.text: (4) giving such other information as is necessary to satisfy the Court that the applicant is entitled to proceed to execution on the judgment or order in question, and that the person against whom it is sought to issue execution is liable to execution on it.

8250.text: The Court hearing such application may grant permission in accordance with the application or may order that any issue or question, a decision on which is necessary to determine the rights of the parties, be tried in any manner in which any question of fact or law arising in proceedings may be tried and, in either case, may impose such terms as to costs or otherwise as it thinks just.

8251.text: Application For Permission To Issue Writ Of Sequestration

8253.text: Notwithstanding anything in Rules 48.29 to 48.35, an application for permission to issue a writ of sequestration must be made in accordance with Part 23 and be heard by a Judge.

8255.text: Subject to Rule 48.38, the application notice , stating the grounds of the application and accompanied by a copy of the witness statement or affidavit in support of the application, must be served personally on the person against whose property it is sought to issue the writ.

8257.text: The Court may dispense with service of the application notice under Rule 48.37 if it thinks it just to do so.

8259.text: The Judge hearing an application for permission to issue a writ of sequestration may sit in private in any case in which, if the application were for an order of committal, he would be entitled to do so by virtue of Rule 52.28 but, except in such a case, the application shall be heard in public.

8262.text: Issue of a writ of execution takes place on its being sealed by the Court .

8264.text: The application for a writ of execution must be signed by or on behalf of the legal representative of the person entitled to execution or, if that person is acting in person, by him.

8266.text: No such order shall be sealed unless at the time tendering it for sealing:

8268.text: (a) the judgment or order in respect of it the order of execution is made, or an authorised copy; (b) where the order may not be made without the permission of the Court , the order granting permission or evidence of the granting of it; and

8269.text: (2) the Court Officer authorised to seal it is satisfied that the period, if any, specified in the judgment or order for the payment of any money or the doing of any other act thereunder has expired.

8271.text: Every order of execution shall bear the date of the day on which it is sealed.

8272.text: Duration And Renewal Of Writ Of Execution

8274.text: For the purpose of execution, a writ of execution is valid in the first instance for 12 months beginning with the date of its issue.

8276.text: Subject to Rule 48.46, where an order of execution has not been wholly executed, the Court may by order extend the validity of the order from time to time for a period of 12 months at any one time beginning with the day on which the order is made, if an application for extension is made to the Court before the day next following that on which the order would otherwise expire or such later day, if any, as the Court may allow.

8278.text: The Court shall not make an order under Rule 48.45 or 48.50 to enforce a judgment or order after 6 years have elapsed from the date when the judgment or order was made.

8280.text: Before an order, the validity of which had been extended under Rule 48.45, is executed either the order must be sealed by the Court showing the date on which the order extending its validity was made or the applicant for the order must serve a sealed notice in Form P48/06 on the relevant enforcement officer informing him of the making of the order and the date thereof.

8282.text: The priority of an order of execution, the validity of which has been extended under Rule 48.45, shall be determined by reference to the date on which it was originally delivered to the relevant enforcement officer

8284.text: The production of a writ of execution , or of such a notice as is mentioned in Rule 48.47 purporting in either case to be sealed as mentioned in that Rule, shall be evidence that the validity of that order of execution, or, as the case may be, of the order referred to in that notice, has been extended under Rule 48.45.

8286.text: Subject to Rule 48.46, if during the validity of an order of execution, an interpleader claim is issued in relation to an execution under that order, the validity of the order shall be extended until the expiry of 12 months from the conclusion of the interpleader proceedings.

8289.text: Any party at whose instance or against whom an order of execution was issued, may serve a notice on the relevant enforcement officer requiring him, within such time as may be specified in the notice, to indorse on the order a statement of the manner in which he has executed it and to send to that party a copy of the statement.

8291.text: If an enforcement officer on whom such a notice is served fails to comply with it, the party by whom it was served may apply to the Court for an order directing the enforcement officer to comply with the notice.

8292.text: Power To Stay Execution Against Assets

8294.text: Where a judgment is given or an order made for the payment by any person of money, and the Court is satisfied, on an application made at the time of the judgment or order , or at any time thereafter, by the judgment debtor or other party liable to execution:

8295.text: (1) that there are special circumstances which render it inexpedient to enforce the judgment or order ; or

8296.text: (2) that the applicant is unable from any cause to pay the money;

8297.text: then, the Court may by order stay the execution against assets of the judgment or order either absolutely or for such period and subject to such conditions as the Court thinks fit.

8299.text: An application under Rule 48.53, if not made at the time the judgment is given or order made, must be made in accordance with Part 23, and may be so made notwithstanding that the party liable to execution did not acknowledge service of the claim form or serve a defence or take any previous part in the proceedings.

8301.text: The grounds on which an application under Rule 48.53 is made must be set out in the application notice and be supported by an affidavit made by or on behalf of the applicant substantiating the grounds and, in particular, where the application is made on the grounds of the applicant’s inability to pay, disclosing his income, the nature and value of any property of his and the amount of any other liabilities of his.

8303.text: The application notice and a copy of the supporting affidavit must be served on the party entitled to enforce the judgment or order not less than 4 clear days before the hearing.

8305.text: An order staying execution under Rule 48.53 may be varied or revoked by a subsequent order.

8306.text: Separate Orders To Enforce Payment Of Costs

8308.text: Where only the payment of money, together with costs to be assessed in accordance with Part 40 (detailed costs assessment), is adjudged or ordered, then, if when the money becomes payable under the judgment or order the costs have not been assessed, the party entitled to enforce that judgment or order may issue a writ for execution against assets to enforce payment of the sum (other than for costs) adjudged or ordered and, not less than 8 days after that order is made, he may obtain a second writ to enforce payment of the assessed costs.

8310.text: A party entitled to enforce a judgment or order for the delivery of possession of any property (other than money) may, if he so elects, obtain a separate writ to enforce payment of any damages or costs awarded to him by that judgment or order .

8311.text: Order For Sale Otherwise Than By Auction

8313.text: An order of the Court under Article 45(3) of the Law of Damages emedies 2005 that a sale of goods seized under an execution may be made otherwise than by public auction may be made on the application of:

8314.text: (1) the judgment creditor ; (2) the judgment debtor ; or

8317.text: An application under Rule 48.60 must be made in accordance with Part 23 and the application notice must contain a short statement of the grounds of the application.

8319.text: Where the applicant for an order under this Rule 48.60 is not the enforcement officer , the enforcement officer must, on the demand of the applicant, send to the applicant a list stating:

8320.text: (1) whether he has notice of the issue of another writ or writs of execution against the goods of the judgment debtor ; and

8321.text: (2) so far as is known to him, the name and address of every creditor who has obtained the issue of another writ of execution ,

8322.text: and where the enforcement officer is the applicant, he must prepare such a list.

8324.text: Not less than 4 clear days before the hearing, the applicant must serve the application notice on each of the other persons by whom the application might have been made and on every person named in the list under Rule 48.62.

8326.text: The applicant must produce the list under Rule 48.62 to the Court on the hearing of the application.

8328.text: Every person on whom the application notice was served may attend and be heard on the hearing of the application.

8329.text: PART 49 Court’s Power To Appoint A Receiver

8331.text: Scope Of This Part And Interpretation

8332.text: 49.1 This Part contains provisions about the Court’s power to appoint a receiver .

8334.text: In this Part ‘receiver’ includes a manager.

8337.text: The Court’s powers to appoint a receiver are set out in:

8340.text: The Court may appoint a receiver :

8341.text: (1) before proceedings have started; (2) in existing proceedings; or (3) on or after judgment .

8343.text: The Court will normally only consider an application for the appointment of a receiver before proceedings are started after notice of the application has been served.

8345.text: Where a judgment creditor applies for the appointment of a receiver as a method of enforcing a judgment , in considering whether to make the appointment the Court will have regard at all times to:

8346.text: (1) the sum owed; (2) the likelihood of making a substantial recovery; and (3) the cost of appointing the receiver .

8348.text: The Court may at any time:

8349.text: (1) terminate the appointment of a receiver ; and (2) appoint another receiver in his place.

8350.text: How To Apply For The Appointment Of A Receiver

8352.text: An application for the appointment of a receiver :

8353.text: (1) may be made without notice; and (2) must be supported by written evidence.

8355.text: If a person applies at the same time for:

8356.text: (1) the appointment of a receiver ; and

8358.text: he must use the same claim form or application notice for both applications.

8360.text: The written evidence in support of an application for the appointment of a receiver must:

8361.text:
(1) explain the reasons why the appointment is required;   
(2) give details of the property which it is proposed that the receiver should get in or manage, including estimates of:

8362.text: (a) the value of the property; and (b) the amount of income it is likely to produce;

8363.text: (3) if the application is to appoint a receiver by way of equitable execution, give details of:

8364.text: (a) the judgment which the applicant is seeking to enforce; (b) the extent to which the debtor has failed to comply with the judgment ; (c) the result of any steps already taken to enforce the judgment ; and (d) why the judgment cannot be enforced by any other method; and

8365.text: (4) if the applicant is asking the Court to allow the receiver to act:

8366.text: (a) without giving security; or (b) before he has given security or satisfied the Court that he has security in place; explain the reasons why that is necessary.

8368.text: In addition, the written evidence should normally identify an individual whom the Court is to be asked to

8369.text: appoint as receiver (‘the nominee’), and should:

8370.text: (1) state the name, address and position of the nominee; (2) include written evidence by a person who knows the nominee, stating that he believes the nominee is a suitable person to be appointed as receiver , and the basis of that belief; and (3) be accompanied by written consent, signed by the nominee, to act as receiver if appointed.

8372.text: If the applicant does not nominate a person to be appointed as receiver , or if the Court decides not to appoint the nominee, the Court may:

8373.text: (1) order that a suitable person be appointed as receiver ; and (2) direct any party to nominate a suitable individual to be appointed.

8375.text: A party directed to nominate a person to be appointed as receiver must file written evidence containing the information required by Rule 49.11 and accompanied by the written consent of the nominee.

8378.text: An order appointing a receiver must be served by the party who applied for it on:

8379.text:
(1) the person appointed as receiver ;   
(2) unless the Court orders otherwise, every other party to the proceedings; and (3) such other persons as the Court may direct.

8381.text: 49.15 The Court may give directions to the receiver when it appoints him or at any time afterwards.

8383.text: The Court will normally, when it appoints a receiver , give directions in relation to security.

8385.text: Other matters about which the Court may give directions include:

8386.text:
(1) whether, and on what basis, the receiver is to be remunerated for carrying out his functions;   
(2) the preparation and service of accounts;

8387.text: (3) the payment of money into Court ; and (4) authorising the receiver to carry on an activity or incur an expense.

8390.text: The Court may direct that before a receiver begins to act or within a specified time he must either:

8391.text: (1) give such security as the Court may determine; or (2) file and serve on all parties to the proceedings evidence that he already has in force sufficient security; to cover his liability for his acts and omissions as a receiver .

8393.text: An order appointing a receiver will normally specify the date by which the receiver must:

8394.text: (1) give security; or (2) file and serve evidence to satisfy the Court that he already has security in force.

8396.text: Unless the Court directs otherwise, security will be given by a guarantee.

8398.text: Where the Court has given directions about giving security, then a guarantee should be prepared in a form, and entered into with a bank or insurance company, approved by the Court .

8400.text: The Court may terminate the appointment of the receiver if he fails to:

8401.text:
(1) give the security; or   
(2) satisfy the Court as to the security he has in force, and (3) by the date specified.

8404.text: The receiver may apply to the Court at any time for directions to assist him in carrying out his function as a receiver .

8406.text: The Court , when it gives directions, may also direct the receiver to serve on any person:

8407.text: (1) the directions; and (2) the application for directions.

8409.text: An application by a receiver for directions may be made by filing an application notice in accordance with Part 23.

8411.text: If the directions sought by the receiver are unlikely to be contentious or important to the parties, he may make the application by letter, and the Court may reply by letter. In such cases the receiver need not serve his letter or the Court’s reply on the parties, unless the Court orders him to do so.

8413.text: Where a receiver applies for directions by letter, the Court may direct him to file and serve an application notice .

8416.text: A receiver may only charge for his services if the Court:

8417.text: (1) so directs; and (2) specifies the basis on which the receiver is to be remunerated.

8420.text: (1) who is to be responsible for paying the receiver ; and (2) the fund or property from which the receiver is to recover his remuneration.

8422.text: If the Court directs that the amount of a receiver’s remuneration is to be determined by the Court:

8423.text: (1) the receiver may not recover any remuneration for his services without a determination by the Court ; and

8424.text: (2) the receiver or any party may apply at any time for such a determination to take place.

8426.text: Unless the Court orders otherwise, in determining the remuneration of a receiver the Court shall award such sum as is reasonable and proportionate in all the circumstances and which takes into account:

8429.text: An application by a receiver for the amount of his remuneration to be determined must be supported by:

8431.text: (a) on what basis the remuneration is claimed; and (b) that it is justified and in accordance with this Part; and

8432.text: (2) a certificate signed by the receiver that he considers that the remuneration he claims is reasonable and proportionate.

8434.text: The Court may, before determining the amount of a receiver’s remuneration:

8435.text: (1) require the receiver to provide further information in support of his claim; and (2) appoint an assessor under Part 31 to assist the Court .

8437.text: The Court may refer the determination of a receiver’s remuneration to the Registrar.

8439.text: Rules 49.28 to 49.34 do not apply to expenses incurred by a receiver in carrying out his functions. These are accounted for as part of his account for the assets he has recovered, and not dealt with as part of the determination of his remuneration.

8442.text: The Court may order a receiver to prepare and serve accounts.

8444.text: A party served with such accounts may apply for an order permitting him to inspect any document in the possession of the receiver relevant to those accounts.

8446.text: A party should not apply for an order under Rule 49.37 without first asking the receiver to permit inspection without an order.

8448.text: Where the Court makes an order under Rule 49.37, it will normally direct that the receiver must:

8449.text: (1) permit inspection within 7 days after being served with the order; and

8450.text: (2) provide a copy of any documents the subject of the order within 7 days after receiving a request for a copy from the party permitted to inspect them, provided that party has undertaken to pay the reasonable cost of making and providing the copy.

8452.text: Any party may, within 14 days of being served with the accounts, serve notice on the receiver :

8453.text: (1) specifying any item in the accounts to which he objects;

8454.text: (2) giving the reason for such objection; and

8455.text: (3) requiring the receiver , within 14 days of receipt of the notice, either:

8456.text: (a) to notify all the parties who were served with the accounts that he accepts the objection; or

8457.text: (b) if he does not accept the objection, to apply for an examination of the accounts in relation to the contested item.

8459.text: When the receiver applies for the examination of the accounts he must at the same time file:

8461.text: (2) a copy of the notice served on him under Rule 49.40.

8463.text: If the receiver fails to comply with Rule 49.40(3), any party may apply to the Court for an examination of the accounts in relation to the contested item.

8465.text: At the conclusion of its examination of the accounts the Court will certify the result.

8467.text: When the Court gives directions under Rule 49.36 for the receiver to prepare and serve accounts, it may:

8468.text: (1) direct the receiver to prepare and serve accounts either by a specified date or at specified intervals; and

8469.text: (2) specify the persons on whom he must serve the accounts.

8471.text: A party should not apply for an order under Rule 49.37 permitting him to inspect documents in the possession of the receiver , without first asking the receiver to permit such inspection without an order.

8474.text: If a receiver fails to comply with any Rule, Practice Direction or direction of the Court the Court may order him to attend a hearing to explain his non-compliance.

8476.text: At the hearing the Court may make any order it considers appropriate, including:

8477.text: (1) terminating the appointment of the receiver ; (2) reducing the receiver’s remuneration or disallowing it altogether; and (3) ordering the receiver to pay the costs of any party.

8480.text: (1) the Court has ordered a receiver to pay a sum of money into Court ; and

8481.text: (2) the receiver has failed to do so;

8482.text: the Court may order him to pay interest on that sum for the time he is in default at such rate as it considers appropriate.

8485.text: A receiver or any party may apply for the receiver to be discharged on completion of his duties.

8487.text: The application notice must be served on the persons who were required under Rule 49.14 to be served with the order appointing the receiver .

8488.text: Order Discharging Or Terminating Appointment Of Receiver

8490.text: An order discharging or terminating the appointment of a receiver may:

8491.text: (1) require him to pay into Court any money held by him; or (2) specify the person to whom he must pay any money or transfer any assets still in his possession; and (3) make provision for the discharge or cancellation of any guarantee given by the receiver as security.

8493.text: The order must be served on the persons who were required under Rule 49.14 to be served with the order appointing the receiver .

8494.text: PART 50 Orders To Obtain Information From Judgment Debtors

8496.text: Scope Of This Part And Interpretation

8498.text: This Part contains rules which provide for a judgment debtor to be required to attend Court to provide information, for the purpose of enabling a judgment creditor to enforce a judgment or order against him.

8501.text: A judgment creditor may apply for an order requiring:

8502.text: (1) a judgment debtor ; or (2) if a judgment debtor is a company or other corporation, an officer of that body; to attend Court to provide information about: (a) the judgment debtor’s means; or

8503.text: (b) any other matter about which information is needed to enforce a judgment or order .

8505.text: An application under Rule 50.2 may be made without notice.

8507.text: The application must be made by filing an application notice in Form P50/01 if the application is to question an individual judgment debtor , or Form P50/02 if the application is to question an officer of a company or other corporation.

8510.text: (1) state the name and address of the judgment debtor ;

8511.text: (2) identify the judgment or order which the judgment creditor is seeking to enforce;

8512.text: (3) if the application is to enforce a judgment or order for the payment of money , state the amount presently owed by the judgment debtor under the judgment or order ;

8513.text: (4) if the judgment debtor is a company or other corporation, state:

8514.text: (a) the name and address of the officer of that body whom the judgment creditor wishes to be ordered to attend Court ; and

8516.text: (5) if the judgment creditor wishes the questioning to be conducted before a Judge, state this and give his reasons;

8517.text: (6) if the judgment creditor wishes the judgment debtor (or other person to be questioned) to be ordered to produce specific documents at Court , identify those documents; and

8518.text: (7) if the application is to enforce a judgment or order which is not for the payment of money, identify the matters about which the judgment creditor wishes the judgment debtor (or officer of the judgment debtor ) to be questioned.

8520.text: An application under Rule 50.2 may be dealt with by the Registrar without a hearing.

8522.text: If the application notice complies with Rules 50.4 and 50.5, an order to attend Court will be issued in the terms of Rule 50.9.

8525.text: (1) may, in any appropriate case, refer an application under Rule 50.2 to a Judge ; and

8526.text: (2) will refer it to a Judge for consideration, if the judgment creditor requests the judgment debtor (or officer of the judgment debtor ) to be questioned before a Judge .

8528.text: A person served with an order issued under Rule 50.7 must:

8529.text:
(1) attend Court at the time and place specified in the order;   
(2) when he does so, produce at Court documents in his control which are described in the order; and (3) answer on oath such questions as the Court may require.

8531.text: The order will normally provide for questioning to take place before the Registrar . The order will provide for questioning to take place before a Judge only if the Judge considering the request decides that there are compelling reasons to make such an order.

8533.text: An order under Rule 50.7 will contain a notice in the following terms:

8534.text: “You must obey this order. If you do not, you may be fined for contempt of Court.”

8537.text: An order to attend Court must, unless the Court otherwise orders, be served personally on the person ordered to attend Court not less than 14 days before the hearing.

8539.text: Service of an order to attend Court for questioning must be carried out by the judgment creditor (or someone acting on his behalf).

8541.text: The judgment creditor must inform the Court not less than 7 days before the date of the hearing if he has been unable to serve the order to attend Court .

8544.text: A person ordered to attend Court may, within 7 days of being served with the order, ask the judgment creditor to pay him a sum reasonably sufficient to cover his travelling expenses to and from Court .

8546.text: The judgment creditor must pay such a sum if requested.

8549.text: The judgment creditor must file an affidavit or affidavits :

8550.text: (1) by the person who served the order giving details of how and when it was served;

8552.text: (a) the person ordered to attend Court has not requested payment of his travelling expenses; or

8553.text: (b) the judgment creditor has paid a sum in accordance with such a request; and

8554.text: (3) stating how much of the judgment debt remains unpaid.

8557.text: (1) file the affidavit or affidavits not less than 2 days before the hearing; or (2) produce it or them at the hearing.

8560.text: The person ordered to attend Court will be questioned on oath.

8562.text: The questioning will be carried out by the Registrar unless the Court has ordered that the hearing shall be before a Judge .

8564.text: The Registrar will ask a standard series of questions, as set out forms in Schedules A and B to this Part. The form in Schedule A will be used if the person being questioned is the judgment debtor , and the form in Schedule B will be used if the person is an officer of a company or other corporation.

8566.text: The judgment creditor or his representative may either:

8567.text: (1) attend Court and ask questions himself or through his legal representative ; or

8568.text: (2) request the Registrar to ask additional questions, by attaching a list of proposed additional questions

8572.text: (1) make a written record of the evidence given, unless the proceedings are tape recorded;

8573.text: (2) at the end of the questioning, read the record of evidence to the person being questioned and ask him to sign it; and

8574.text: (3) if the person refuses to sign it, note that refusal on the record of evidence.

8576.text: Where the hearing takes places before a Judge , the judgment creditor or his legal representative must attend and conduct the questioning, and the standard questions in the forms in Schedules A and B will not be used.

8578.text: Where the hearing takes places before a Judge , the proceedings will be tape recorded and the Court will not make a written record of the evidence.

8581.text: If the hearing is adjourned, the Court will give directions as to the manner in which notice of the new hearing is to be served on the judgment debtor .

8584.text: Where a judgment debtor was ordered to attend before the Registrar , if a person against whom an order has been made under Rule 50.7:

8585.text: (1) fails to attend Court ; (2) refuses at the hearing to take the oath or to answer any question; or (3) otherwise fails to comply with the order; the Registrar will refer the matter to a Judge .

8587.text: If the Registrar refers to a Judge the failure of a judgment debtor to comply with an order under Rule 50.7, he will certify in writing the respect in which the judgment debtor failed to comply with the order.

8589.text: That Judge may, subject to Rules 50.31 and 50.32, make a committal order against the person.

8591.text: Where a judgment debtor was ordered to attend before a Judge , if a person against whom an order has been made under Rule 50.7:

8592.text: (1) fails to attend Court ; (2) refuses at the hearing to take the oath or to answer any question; or (3) otherwise fails to comply with the order; the Judge may, subject to Rules 50.31 and 50.32, make a committal order against the person.

8594.text: A committal order for failing to attend Court may not be made unless the judgment creditor has complied with Rules 50.15 to 50.18.

8596.text: If a committal order is made, the Judge will direct that:

8597.text: (1) the order shall be suspended provided that the person:

8598.text: (a) attends Court at a time and place specified in the order; and (b) complies with all the terms of that order and the original order; and

8599.text: (2) if the person fails to comply with any term on which the committal order is suspended, he shall be brought before a Judge to consider whether the committal order should be discharged.

8601.text: Where Rule 50.32 applies, the appointment specified will be:

8602.text:
(1) before a Judge , if: (a) the original order under Rule 50.7 was to attend before a Judge ; or (b) the Judge making the suspended committal order so directs; and   
(2) otherwise, before the Registrar .

8604.text: Rules 50.12 to 50.14 (service of order), and Rules 50.17(1) and 50.18 (affidavit of service ), apply with the necessary changes to a suspended committal order as they do to an order to attend Court .

8610.text: (1) ‘enforcement officer’ means the Court Bailiff or an individual who is authorised to act as such by the Chief Justice or a person acting on his behalf;

8611.text: (2) ‘order of execution’ includes an order of execution against assets, an order of possession, an order of delivery, or an order of sequestration and any further order in aid of any of the aforementioned orders.

8612.text: Entitlement To Relief By Way Of Interpleader

8615.text: (1) a person is under a liability in respect of a debt or in respect of any assets and he is, or expects to be, sued for or in respect of that debt or assets by two or more persons making adverse claims thereto; or

8616.text: (2) claim is made to any assets taken or intended to be taken by an enforcement officer in execution under any process, or to the proceeds or value of any such assets, by a person other than the person against whom the process is issued;

8617.text: the person under liability as mentioned in sub-paragraph (1) or (subject to Rules 51.3 to 51.6) an enforcement officer may apply to the Court for relief by way of interpleader.

8618.text: Claim To Assets Taken In Execution

8620.text: Any person making a claim to or in respect of any assets taken or intended to be taken in execution under process of the Court , or to the proceeds or value of any such assets, must give notice of his claim to the enforcement officer charged with the execution of the process and must include in his notice a statement of his address, and that address shall be his address for service .

8623.text: On receipt of a claim made under Rule 51.3 the enforcement officer must forthwith give notice thereof to the execution creditor and the execution creditor must, within 7 days after receiving the notice, give notice to the enforcement officer informing him whether he admits or disputes the claim. An execution creditor who gives notice in accordance with this Rule admitting a claim shall only be liable to the enforcement officer for any fees and expenses incurred by the enforcement officer before receipt of that notice.

8626.text: (1) the enforcement officer receives a notice from an execution creditor under Rule 51.4 disputing a claim, or the execution creditor fails, within the period mentioned in that Rule, to give the required notice; and

8627.text: (2) the claim made under Rule 51.3 is not withdrawn;

8628.text: the enforcement officer may apply to the Court for relief under this Part.

8630.text: An enforcement officer who receives a notice from an execution creditor under Rule 51.4 admitting a claim made under Rule 51.3 shall withdraw from possession of the assets claimed and may apply to the Court for an order restraining the bringing of a claim against him for or in respect of his having taken possession of the assets.

8631.text: Claim In Respect Of Assets Protected From Seizure

8633.text: Where a judgment debtor whose assets have been seized, or are intended to be seized, by an enforcement officer under an order of execution claims that such assets are not liable to execution by virtue of Article 45(2) of the Lawof Damages and Remedies 2005, he must within 5 days of the seizure give notice in writing to the enforcement officer identifying all those assets in respect of which he makes such a claim and the grounds of such claim in respect of each item.

8635.text: Upon receipt of a notice of claim under Rule 51.7, the enforcement officer must forthwith give notice thereof to the execution creditor and to any person who has made a claim to, or in respect of, the goods under Rule 51.3 and the execution creditor and any person who has made claim must, within 7 days of receipt of such notice, inform the enforcement officer in writing whether he admits or disputes the judgment debtor’s claim in respect of each item.

8637.text: The enforcement officer shall withdraw from possession of any goods in respect of which the judgment debtor’s claim is admitted or if the execution creditor or any person claiming under Rule 51.3 fails to notify him in accordance with Rule 51.8 and the enforcement officer shall so inform the parties in writing.

8639.text: Where the enforcement officer receives notice from:

8641.text: (2) any such person to whom notice was given under Rule 51.8;

8642.text: that the claim or any part thereof is disputed, he must forthwith seek the directions of the Court and may include therein an application for an order restraining the bringing of any claim against him for, or in respect of, his having seized any of those assets or his having failed so to do.

8644.text: The enforcement officer’s application for directions under Rule 51.10 shall be made by an application in accordance with Part 23 and, on the hearing of the application, the Court may:

8645.text: (1) determine the judgment debtor’s claim immediately; or

8646.text: (2) give such directions for the determination of any issue raised by such claim as may be just.

8648.text: The Registrar shall have power to make an order of the kind referred to in Rule 51.11.

8651.text: An application for relief under this Part must be made by claim form in accordance with Part 8 unless made in an existing claim, in which case it must be made in accordance with Part 23.

8653.text: Where the applicant is an enforcement officer who has withdrawn from possession of assets taken in execution and who is applying for relief under Rule 51.6 the claim form must be served on any person who made a claim under Rule 51.3 to or in respect of those assets, and that person may attend the hearing of the application.

8655.text: Subject to Rule 51.16 a claim form or application notice under Rule 51.13 must be supported by evidence that the applicant:

8656.text: (1) claims no interest in the subject matter in dispute other than for charges or costs;

8657.text: (2) does not collude with any of the claimants to that subject matter; and

8658.text: (3) is willing to pay or transfer that subject matter into Court or to dispose of it as the Court may direct.

8660.text: Where the applicant is an enforcement officer , he shall not provide such evidence as is referred to in Rule 51.15 unless directed by the Court to do so.

8662.text: Any person who makes a claim under Rule 51.3 and who is served with a claim form under Rule 51.14 shall within 14 days serve on the execution creditor and the sheriff an affidavit specifying and/or describing any assets claimed and setting out the grounds upon which such claim is based.

8664.text: Where the applicant is an enforcement officer a claim form under Rule 51.13 must give notice of the requirement in Rule 51.17.

8665.text: Powers Of Court Hearing The Claim

8667.text: Where on the hearing of a claim under this Part all the persons by whom adverse claims to the subjectmatter in dispute (hereafter in this Part referred to as ‘the interpleader claimants’) appear, the Court may order:

8668.text: (1) that any interpleader claimant be made a defendant in any claim pending with respect to the subject-matter in dispute in substitution for or in addition to the applicant for relief under this Part; or

8669.text: (2) that an issue between the interpleader claimants be stated and tried and may direct which of the interpleader claimants is to be claimants and which defendant .

8672.text: (1) the applicant under this Part is an enforcement officer ;

8673.text: (2) all the interpleader claimants consent or any of them so requests; or

8674.text: (3) the question at issue between the interpleader claimants is a question of law and the facts are not in dispute;

8675.text: the Court may immediately determine the question at issue between the interpleader claimants and make an order accordingly on such terms as may be just.

8677.text: Where an interpleader claimant, having been duly served with a claim form under this Part, does not appear at the hearing or, having appeared, fails or refuses to comply with an order made in the proceedings, the Court may make an order declaring the interpleader claimant, and all persons claiming under him, barred from prosecuting his claim against the applicant for such relief and all persons claiming under him, but such an order shall not affect the rights of the interpleader claimants as between

8679.text: Power To Order Sale Of Assets Taken In Execution

8681.text: Where an application for relief under this Part is made by an enforcement officer who has taken possession of any assets in execution under any process, and an interpleader claimant alleges that he is entitled to the assets by way of security for debt, the Court may order those assets or any part thereof to be sold and may direct that the proceeds of sale be applied in such manner and on such terms as may be just and as may be specified in the order.

8684.text: Where a defendant to a claim applies for relief under this Part in the claim, the Court may by order stay all further proceedings in the claim.

8687.text: The Court may in or for the purposes of any interpleader proceedings make such order as to costs or any other matter as it thinks just.

8689.text: Where the interpleader claimant fails to appear at the hearing, the Court may direct that the enforcement officer’s and execution creditor’s costs shall be assessed by the Registrar .

8692.text: Where the Court considers it necessary or expedient to make an order in any interpleader proceedings in several proceedings, the Court may make such an order; and the order shall bear the titles of all those proceedings and shall be binding on all the parties to them.
